children.text: Symptoms we can help with
Cramps Or Pelvic Pain

Cramps or pelvic pain can be uncomfortable, disruptive, and hard to ignore. A visit for these symptoms helps uncover what’s going on, whether it’s cycle-related changes, muscle tension, or an underlying condition, and creates a personalized plan to relieve pain and help you feel more in control of your body.
Abnormal Bleeding

From PMS to PCOS, heavy periods to no periods, we can help you evaluate and manage your cycle. Heavy bleeding, clots, and spotting can be symptoms of an underlying issue, and they are never something you have to just "deal with." We'll work together to diagnose what's causing the abnormal bleeding and find a treatment for you. Stop worrying about your cycle and start working toward a healthier, more predictable flow.
Vaginal Infection

From chronic yeast infections to Bacterial Vaginosis (BV) to that "not-sure-what-but-something-is-up" feeling, we've got you covered with diagnosis, treatment, and prevention. We know these symptoms are frustrating, often recurrent, and rarely discussed in a supportive setting. At Tia, we provide expert care to find the root cause, address imbalances, and create a personalized plan to get you back to feeling comfortable and confident, without judgment.

modules.title: Where you can get dedicated care

children.text: Integrated women’s health, all in one place
The Problem

Healthcare should be a relationship, not a series of disconnected transactions. But all too often, it's not. Gynecological and sexual health is too often siloed, forcing you to go to separate providers for your annual exam, a painful sex consult, or a new birth control prescription. Many women feel isolated and alone, passed from one specialist to the next without a central provider who truly knows their entire health story. We know these frustrations are real. In a 2022 report, nearly
one in three women said they felt dismissed by their doctor.

items.signupSubcopy: We need some basic info to find you the right primary gynecologist and book your first visit.

children.text: Takes 3–5 minutes to create an account • No membership fee to book • Insurance accepted
Tia’s Solution

We believe women deserve care that treats the whole person. Our model ensures your gynecology issues are never treated in isolation. Our providers are trained to connect the dots between symptoms like irregular periods or painful sex and your overall physical, mental, and emotional health. This allows us to offer holistic, trauma-informed care that gets to the root cause of conditions like painful sex, irregular bleeding, chronic pelvic pain . These symptoms often connect back to hormones or stress. Our providers don’t just prescribe birth control; we help you find the option that best fits your body, values, and lifestyle.
This approach helps women get comprehensive care, covering all aspects of their well-being:
Integrated Prevention:
After joining Tia, members complete
life-saving screenings at rates 2–3x the national average
, ensuring your gynecologic health (like Pap tests and cervical cancer screenings) is proactively managed.
Seamless Specialty Referrals:
If you need specialty care, Tia partners with top health systems to coordinate your referral while remaining your primary care home.
Mental Health is GYN Health
88.2% of Tia patients were screened for depression
because we recognize the deep connection between chronic pain, stress, and mental health.

Why Gynecological and Sexual Health matters for women
Your body is a connected system.

Gynecological symptoms aren't isolated; they’re often signals from the rest of your body. A dedicated care team helps connect the dots, understanding how stress, sleep, and overall health can influence things like your period or pelvic pain, sexual wellness, or irregular bleeding.
Your symptoms deserve to be taken seriously.

You're not imagining it. For too long, women's pain—from endometriosis to chronic pelvic issues—has been dismissed and minimized. You deserve care from providers trained to understand the full picture of women's reproductive and sexual health who will listen actively and never brush off your concerns
Prevention is the foundation of lifelong health.

Building a relationship with a trusted primary care team means catching problems early, from getting your cervical cancer screening (Pap test) on time to addressing changes in your reproductive health , ensuring you stay healthier for the long term.

"There is no question too personal, too complicated, or too embarrassing when it comes to your gynecologic and sexual health. Our primary role is to be a compassionate, non-judgmental partner in your total well-being."
Rebecca Reilly, DNP, FNP-C
, Market Medical Director
How Tia is different for Sexual and Gynecological health
✓ Hybrid care, built for modern women
Seamlessly transition between virtual consults for birth control and in-person procedures like IUD insertion.
✓ One record, one plan, one team
Your primary care provider is always in sync with your GYN care, so you never have to repeat your history to get a Pap test, STI screen, or tackle a more complex condition.
✓ Seamless Specialty Access
Tia partners with leading health systems to ensure that if you need specialty care—for complex issues like advanced endometriosis—you are referred and supported while Tia remains your home base.
✓ Holistic/comprehensive care
We use both medication and integrative options (like acupuncture or pelvic floor therapy referrals) for chronic conditions like PCOS or painful sex, aiming to get to the root and give you options.
✓ Care that grows with you
We are your health home for every stage—from your first Pap test to navigating menopause and beyond.

testimonials.Content: My experience at Tia was exceptional from start to finish. As someone with a 9–5 job, being able to book a 6 PM appointment for an OBGYN concern was a huge relief. From check-in, Angie made me feel welcomed and cared for, and Stephanie was professional, gentle with the blood draw, and made sure I felt comfortable the whole time. I saw Jamee, the nurse practitioner, who was incredibly supportive and knowledgeable. She took her time answering all my questions and helped me feel at ease with clear explanations and genuine care. Tia delivers warm, respectful, patient-focused care, and I’ve already started recommending it to friends and colleagues. Here, you truly feel heard and supported.
testimonials.Title: Clarissa B., Tia Patient

testimonials.Content: I had my IUD removed and replaced today which is probably one of the most uncomfortable procedures EVER but the care team made sure that my comfort was prioritized. In addition to the luxurious robe and beautiful interiors I've come to love from Tia, I was provided with a heating pad, white noise machine, and lavender essential oil to smell during my procedure. My NP was extremely thorough and asked if I had any questions and walked me through every step as it was happening. I felt very taken care of! As soon as I got home I was messaged aftercare information! It’s clear that Tia thinks through every step of their care and prioritizes comfort along with exceptional healthcare.
testimonials.Title: Chloe M., Tia Patient

The care is excellent with a focus on the whole person, offering mental health and hormone treatments as well as primary women's healthcare. Used acupuncture with my procedure and couldn't believe the difference it made. 10/10 recommend Tia to anyone

testimonials.Content: I’ve read many reviews about this facility, but my experience was truly exceptional. I’ve had a lot of trauma around women’s health and avoided gynecologic care for years, but Tia completely changed that. Dr. Ryan Darvish is the first provider who made me feel genuinely safe and cared for. After an abnormal pap, I went through several difficult procedures, and he guided me through each one with compassion, clear communication, and a focus on keeping me comfortable. The same care and support were present during my IUD removal and replacement, which he made nearly painless. I’ve never felt so at ease during appointments that once felt overwhelming. Dr. Darvish is truly a gift, and I recommend him to anyone with fear or trauma around gynecologic care. Tia is now the only place I’ll go for my health needs.
testimonials.Title: Emily F., Tia Patient

testimonials.Content: I cannot say enough good things about my experience at Tia Clinic in Studio City, thanks to Jamee and Alauna. Their customer service was truly unmatched—every interaction was thoughtful, kind, and professional. Jamee was extremely patient with me, taking the time to answer every single one of my many questions without hesitation. She made me feel heard and valued, which is so rare these days. Both Jamee and Alauna went above and beyond to make me feel comfortable throughout my visit.  I’ve officially found my gyno for life with Jamee, and I couldn’t be more grateful for the care and support I received. If you’re looking for a team that genuinely cares about their patients, look no further than Tia Clinic.

How do I know if a Perimenopause and Menopause Assessment is right for me?
What is a Perimenopause & Menopause Assessment visit?

The Perimenopause & Menopause Assessment is a comprehensive, whole-body visit with one of Tia’s menopause care specialists. We'll listen to your concerns, review your health history, and work with you to create a personalized care plan for your unique journey through perimenopause or menopause, all with your preferences at the center.
Who should get a  Perimenopause & Menopause Assessment?

Whether you’re curious if you’re in perimenopause or certain you’re in menopause, we are here to care for you. Our holistic approach means that you're always in the driver's seat of your healthcare decisions, no matter where you may be:
Early Perimenopause: When symptoms first begin and may be subtle.
Late Perimenopause: When symptoms become more frequent or severe.
Menopause: When you have not had a period for 12 consecutive months.
Post-Menopause: The years following menopause, where new health concerns can arise.
Symptoms we can support
We provide expert, whole-body care for a wide range of perimenopause and menopause symptoms, including:
Hot flashes
Sleep disturbances
Brain fog and memory issues
Mood changes, anxiety, or irritability
Low libido
Weight changes
Vaginal dryness
Bone health concerns
Cardiometabolic concerns
How this appointment can help:
Understand what’s happening —
connect the dots between your symptoms and this life stage, exploring root causes
Build a personalized plan —
from hormone therapy to acupuncture, with your preferences guiding every decision
Get clear, expert answers —
evidence-based guidance and myth-busting from women’s health specialists
Feel heard and supported —
a care team that listens, validates, and partners with you on next steps
Insurance and Pricing
Is this covered by insurance?
Yes! Tia will bill your insurance. Just like at any doctor’s office, you may be responsible for a co-pay or coinsurance, depending on your health plan.

modules.title: Where to get perimenopause and menopause care

children.text: No, this visit is for anyone in perimenopause, menopause, or postmenopause.

faqs.question: Do I need to be in menopause to book this visit?

children.text: We treat menopause as part of your whole health, addressing other concerns and connecting your care before, during, and after this stage.

faqs.question: What makes Tia’s perimenopause and menopause care different?

children.text: One of Tia’s specialty-trained perimenopause & menopause providers who are experts in women’s health, this life stage, and building meaningful patient relationships.

faqs.question: Who will I see?

children.text: Options may include evidence-based hormone therapy, non-hormonal medications, acupuncture, nutrition, and mental health support.

faqs.question: What treatments do you offer?

children.text: The healthcare industry has long approached perimenopause (the transitional period before menopause) and menopause (the point in which menses has stopped for 1 year or more) with a narrow focus on hormone replacement therapy (HRT). While HRT can effectively address certain symptoms, this reductionist approach fails to account for the complex physiological and psychological changes women experience during this critical life phase. Recent research indicates that we need a more comprehensive, preventive approach to support women's health during perimenopause and beyond.
Understanding the Gaps in Current Care

A 2023 study in the Journal of Women's Health found that nearly 75% of perimenopausal women report receiving insufficient guidance from their healthcare providers about managing this transition. This gap in care is particularly concerning given that perimenopause typically begins in a woman's 40s — a crucial period for preventing various age-related health conditions.

The current trend of virtual-only healthcare solutions that focus solely on prescribing HRT, while convenient, may inadvertently create a false sense of comprehensive care. Research published in Menopause: The Journal of The North American Menopause Society demonstrates that women who receive only HRT without additional preventive care show higher rates of undiagnosed cardiovascular issues and bone density problems compared to those receiving comprehensive care.
Essential Preventive Screening and Monitoring: A Data-Driven Approach

Recent longitudinal studies have demonstrated that comprehensive screening during perimenopause can significantly alter health trajectories. Research published in the Journal of Clinical Endocrinology & Metabolism shows that women who receive regular preventive screening during perimenopause have a 64% lower risk of developing serious cardiovascular complications and a 43% reduction in metabolic disorders over the subsequent decade.
A holistic approach to perimenopausal care must include systematic monitoring of key health markers, with timing and frequency guided by the latest clinical evidence:
Metabolic Health Markers
Comprehensive lipid panel - essential as studies show a 50% increase in cardiovascular risk during the perimenopausal transition
Fasting glucose and HbA1c - particularly important as insulin sensitivity often decreases during this period
Insulin resistance markers - research indicates early detection can prevent up to 70% of perimenopausal-onset diabetes cases
Inflammatory markers including hsCRP - new evidence links inflammation during perimenopause to accelerated aging
Comprehensive Hormonal Panel
Thyroid function tests (TSH, Free T3, Free T4) - particularly crucial as research indicates that 1 in 8 women develop thyroid dysfunction during perimenopause

When appropriate - estradiol and FSH levels to track reproductive aging + DHEA and testosterone levels: new studies show these hormones play a more significant role in cognitive function than previously understood
Bone Health Indicators
Vitamin D levels - studies show optimal levels can reduce bone loss by up to 30%
Calcium and bone-specific alkaline phosphatase
N-telopeptide and other bone turnover markers
DEXA scans starting at age 45 for baseline bone density assessment

Research from the National Osteoporosis Foundation reveals that women lose up to 20% of their bone density in the first five years after menopause. However, new data from the Women's Health Initiative demonstrates that early intervention, beginning in perimenopause, can reduce this loss by up to 65%. A groundbreaking 2023 study in the Journal of Bone and Mineral Research showed that women who received comprehensive bone health management, including regular density screening starting in perimenopause, had a 45% lower risk of osteoporotic fractures later in life.
Mental Health: The Interconnected Impact on Cognitive Function and Quality of Life

The psychological impact of perimenopause extends far beyond mood changes, affecting cognitive function, social relationships, and long-term brain health. Recent research has revealed the profound interconnections between hormonal changes, mental health, and cognitive aging:
Depression and Anxiety
Up to 23% of women experience significant depression during perimenopause, compared to 8% in the general population
New research from the Harvard Study of Moods and Cycles shows that women with no prior history of depression are 2.5 times more likely to develop depressive symptoms during perimenopause
68% of women report anxiety symptoms during this transition, with 51% experiencing their first anxiety episode
Cognitive Function and Long-term Brain Health
Women experiencing perimenopausal depression show a 70% higher risk of developing cognitive decline later in life
New neuroimaging studies reveal that untreated perimenopausal depression is associated with accelerated brain aging and reduced hippocampal volume
Research from the Women's Brain Project indicates that addressing mental health during perimenopause can reduce the risk of developing Alzheimer's disease by up to 35%
Economic and Social Impact
The economic impact of lost productivity due to perimenopausal mental health issues has reached $10.6 billion annually in the US alone
Women who receive integrated mental health support during perimenopause are 45% more likely to maintain their career trajectory
Relationship satisfaction scores improve by 62% when both partners receive education and support during this transition
Innovative Treatment Approaches
Recent clinical trials have demonstrated the effectiveness of integrated treatment approaches that combine:
Traditional psychotherapy with hormone-aware cognitive behavioral therapy
Mindfulness-based stress reduction specifically tailored for perimenopausal symptoms
Group support programs that show a 58% improvement in quality of life metrics
Regular cognitive assessment and early intervention strategies
Women's Vital Role in Society and Economy

The importance of supporting women's health through this transition extends far beyond individual well-being, with significant economic and societal implications. According to the U.S. Department of Labor, women over 50 represent one of the fastest-growing segments of the workforce, with labor force participation rates that have transformed our economy:
67% of women aged 55-64 are actively employed, contributing approximately $1.8 trillion annually to the U.S. GDP
Women over 50 hold 35% of management positions in Fortune 500 companies
The "longevity economy" driven by working women over 50 is projected to reach $28.2 trillion by 2026
Women-owned businesses started by entrepreneurs over 50 employ more than 12 million people in the United States
Beyond their direct economic contributions, women in this age group provide essential unpaid care work that sustains our communities:
70% of grandparents who provide regular childcare are women
Women over 55 contribute an average of 100 volunteer hours per year, 35% more than their male counterparts
Women represent 65% of primary caregivers for aging parents
The economic value of unpaid caregiving by women over 50 is estimated at $470 billion annually

These statistics underscore a critical reality: women's contributions to society and the economy actually increase with age, making it imperative that we support their health through the perimenopausal transition and beyond.
A Call for Comprehensive Care

The evidence is clear: we need a fundamental shift in how we approach perimenopausal care. Companies like Tia are leading the way by offering integrated healthcare solutions that address the full spectrum of women's health needs. This comprehensive approach includes:
Regular preventive screening and monitoring
Personalized hormone management when appropriate
Mental health support and counseling
Nutrition and lifestyle guidance
Bone health screening + intervention

and
in clinic - knowing that choice empowers women and many of these critical interventions can not be done through virtual care alone

As our understanding of the interconnected nature of women's health grows, so too must our approach to care. The current fragmented system of care delivery fails to address the complex needs of women during this critical life phase. By implementing comprehensive care models that address both the physiological and psychological aspects of perimenopause, we can better support women's health throughout their lives.

As we look to the future, it's clear that investing in comprehensive perimenopausal care isn't just about improving individual health outcomes — it's about supporting the backbone of our communities and ensuring that women can continue to thrive in their vital societal roles well into their later years.

children.text: Ch-ch-ch-ch-changes...
When David Bowie sang about changes, he might not have been thinking about menopause, but the theme rings true.
Menopause
is all about change—changes in your body, your mood, your health, and yes, your sex drive.

As women transition through perimenopause, menopause, and postmenopause, many experience a range of physical and emotional symptoms, including changes in libido. But fear not! While menopause can challenge your sexual health, there are many ways to manage these changes and reignite your sex drive before, during, and after menopause.

editorialText.title: The Menopause and Sex Drive Connection

children.text: During menopause, the decline in reproductive hormones like estrogen, progesterone, and testosterone can have a major impact on sexual health. But the experience isn’t the same for everyone. Some women may see little change in their libido, while others notice a significant decrease.

editorialText.title: The Impact of Menopause on Sex Drive and Libido

children.text: Perimenopause (Before Menopause)

The hormonal shifts that begin in perimenopause can lead to a variety of sexual health challenges. As estrogen levels decrease, vaginal dryness, discomfort during sex, and reduced sexual desire are common. Hot flashes and night sweats may leave you feeling exhausted, further affecting your mood and libido. Additionally, many women experience emotional shifts, such as irritability or anxiety, that can reduce interest in sex.

Solution: Managing perimenopause and sex drive requires addressing both the physical and emotional symptoms. Maintaining open communication with your partner, engaging in pelvic floor exercises, and using lubricants or vaginal moisturizers can help improve comfort and libido. Hormone replacement therapy (HRT) may also be an option for some women.
Menopause (The Transition)

Once you’ve gone 12 months without a period, you’ve officially reached menopause. At this stage, estrogen levels are significantly lower, which can lead to further sexual health changes, including vaginal atrophy (thinning and dryness of the vaginal tissues),
which can make sex painful
. Testosterone, which also plays a role in sexual desire, also tends to decline during menopause.

Solution: Hormone replacement therapy (HRT) is a key treatment for many women at this stage. Estrogen therapy can help reduce vaginal dryness and improve sexual comfort. Local treatments, such as vaginal estrogen creams or rings, can target vaginal symptoms directly without affecting the rest of your body.
Postmenopause (After Menopause)

Postmenopause marks the years following your final period, and while many menopause symptoms may ease, some sexual health issues persist. Vaginal dryness can remain, and lower libido can still be a challenge. The emotional impact of aging and shifting body image can also affect your confidence and sexual desire.

Solution: Focusing on overall well-being, such as maintaining a healthy diet, managing stress, and staying active, can help boost your energy and sex drive. Some women find that exploring non-hormonal treatments, such as lubricants, can help make sex more comfortable.
Mental health support
, like therapy, can also help with emotional barriers to intimacy.

editorialText.title: How to Improve Your Sex Drive Before, During, and After Menopause

children.text: Here are practical strategies to enhance your libido and sexual satisfaction at every stage of menopause:
Hormone Replacement Therapy (HRT)

HRT can be a game-changer for women experiencing significant changes in sex drive due to hormonal fluctuations. Estrogen therapy can relieve vaginal dryness and discomfort, while testosterone therapy may help boost libido. For women who are not candidates for systemic HRT, local treatments (such as vaginal estrogen creams or rings) can target symptoms like vaginal atrophy without affecting the entire body.
Pelvic Floor Exercises (Kegels)

Strengthening your pelvic floor muscles with Kegel exercises can improve sexual function and sensation, particularly after menopause. These exercises help improve blood flow to the genital area, which can enhance arousal and make orgasm easier to achieve. Regular pelvic floor exercises can also help reduce urinary incontinence, another common issue after menopause.
Lubricants and Vaginal Moisturizers

Vaginal dryness can be a major turn-off during menopause. To make sex more comfortable, use a water-based lubricant or vaginal moisturizer before and during intercourse. These products can help alleviate discomfort caused by vaginal atrophy and improve sexual pleasure.
Open Communication with Your Partner

Communication is key to a healthy sexual relationship during menopause. Be open with your partner about any changes you’re experiencing. Discussing your desires, boundaries, and concerns in a nonjudgmental way can foster intimacy and help you both find new ways to connect.
Prioritize Mental Health

Emotional well-being plays a huge role in sexual health. Managing stress, addressing feelings of anxiety or depression, and working on body image can all have positive effects on libido. Seeking therapy or counseling to address these emotional shifts can help improve your relationship with your body and your sexual identity.
Non-Hormonal Options

If you’re not interested in hormone therapy, there are still options available to improve sexual health. Over-the-counter vaginal moisturizers can help maintain moisture in the vaginal area, and lubricants can reduce discomfort during intercourse. For women who experience anxiety or low mood, mindfulness, relaxation techniques, or even cognitive-behavioral therapy (CBT) can help improve mental and emotional barriers to sex.
Explore New Forms of Intimacy

Sex isn’t the only way to connect with your partner. Many women find that exploring other forms of intimacy, such as massage, cuddling, or focusing on foreplay, can strengthen emotional bonds and reignite desire. Engaging in new forms of erotic play, like using erotic audio or fantasizing together, can also add excitement back into your sexual life.

editorialText.title: What to Expect from Your Sex Drive After Menopause

children.text: Postmenopausal women often report less frequent sexual desire or intimacy, but that doesn't mean their sex lives have to be over. Many women experience a resurgence in sexual desire once they’ve adjusted to the changes in their bodies and life. If you’re proactive about managing menopause symptoms and communicating with your partner, there’s no reason why you can’t continue to have a fulfilling, satisfying sex life well beyond menopause.

Menopause can bring significant changes to your sex drive and sexual health, but it doesn’t mean the end of intimacy or pleasure. By understanding the impact of menopause on libido and taking proactive steps—such as using hormone therapy, practicing pelvic floor exercises, and prioritizing emotional well-being—you can maintain or even enhance your sexual health during this stage of life.
Navigating the changes of menopause can be challenging, but you don’t have to do it alone.
Join Tia
, where you’ll find a team of healthcare experts expert advice, and valuable resources to help you manage menopause with confidence—especially when it comes to maintaining a fulfilling sex life.

children.text: There’s nothing glamorous about hot flashes. The most telling symptom of menopause and the most common reason people seek treatment for this condition, hot flashes (aka vasomotor symptoms) are sudden feelings of warmth in the upper body, which usually feel most intense over the chest, neck, and face.
But there is no shortage of better (and probably more useful) descriptions offered up by women who’ve felt the heat of hot flashes first-hand:
'It’s like my own personal summer.”
“It feels like being consumed by fire from the inside out.”
“It’s like having a furnace explode in my body.”

The medical term is so infamous, it has spawned an entire industry of self-help products and merch. There are hot flash t-shirts (“Still hot – it just comes in flashes now!”). Hot flash pajamas. Hot flash spray. Hot flash sheets. Hot flash patches. Hot flash tea. Hot flash fans.

Of course, hot flashes are just the beginning of things. There are several other symptoms menopausal people often feel on the regular. Night sweats. Mood swings. Painful intercourse. Low libido. Vaginal dryness. Irregular periods. Heavy bleeding. Sleep disturbances. And the list, literally, goes on.

It goes without saying that perimenopause (the time period that precedes menopause when symptoms first appear) and menopause are naturally-occurring conditions that can be unnaturally disruptive, debilitating, and difficult to navigate.
Thankfully,
menopause is having a moment

and, hopefully, a moment that will last. Media outlets, social media influencers, and medical practitioners are paying more attention. And many forward-thinking health and wellness companies are developing new products to help educate and ease the minds and bodies of the 2+ million people who reach menopause each year in the US.

Hopefully, this overview will also educate and ease a few minds, and help demystify and destigmatize a natural and unavoidable bodily process that should be respected as a rite of passage worth honoring and understanding.

editorialText.title: What is menopause?

children.text: Menopause

is a natural biological process that marks the end of a person’s menstrual cycles and fertility, and its diagnosis is confirmed after an individual has gone 12 consecutive months without a menstrual cycle. At this stage, ovaries stop producing estrogen and stop releasing eggs. The time period leading up to menopause, when people start experiencing changes in their menstrual cycles, hot flashes, and other symptoms is called
perimenopause.

As perimenopause marches toward menopause, many people start experiencing more and more menopause symptoms (including irregular periods), but ovulation still occurs and a perimenopausal person can still get pregnant.
Are you experiencing symptoms of menopause and need medical support? Join Tia and schedule an appointment at the clinic nearest you.
Located in New York, Phoenix, San Francisco, and Los Angeles.

editorialText.title: How long does perimenopause last? When does menopause begin?

children.text: The average duration of perimenopause is around four years, although it can last just a few months or extend as long as a decade. Characterized by irregular menstrual cycles and other symptoms related to hormonal fluctuations, perimenopause usually starts in your 40s. Some people experience symptoms for a few months. Some people have symptoms for several years. Typically, menopause begins in a person’s late 40s or early 50s, but the exact age varies widely.
The average age of onset of menopause is 51.
Menopause (and menopausal symptoms) can also be prematurely triggered by having a hysterectomy or having the ovaries surgically removed.

Many factors, which are notoriously difficult to identify and predict, can influence the timing of menopause, including genetics, reproductive history, certain health conditions and treatments (cancer therapy), and some lifestyle habits (smoking).
Did you know?
The average age of menopause onset is typically between 40 to 58 years old with an average age of onset being 51 years old, although this can vary significantly from person to person.

editorialText.title: What are the symptoms of menopause and perimenopause?

children.text: Menopause is associated with a variety of symptoms, but the duration and severity of these symptoms vary among people. Additionally, some symptoms will be temporary and resolve on their own while others may persist and require medical attention. Every situation is specific and anyone experiencing bothersome menopausal symptoms should
consult her healthcare provider
to discuss management options.

In some cases, your provider might recommend a blood test to check your follicle-stimulating hormone (FSH) and estradiol (E2) levels. Although a high FSH can be a sign of perimenopause, a single FSH reading isn’t a reliable indicator since day-to-day hormone levels fluctuate dramatically. Truthfully, it can be difficult to distinguish the hormonally-based symptoms of perimenopause from more general changes due to life transitions – aging, midlife events, children leaving home, career and relationship changes – so it’s unlikely all perimenopausal symptoms are due to hormonal fluctuations alone.
Common symptoms of menopause include:
Irregular Menstrual Cycles:

Declining estrogen levels can make periods more unpredictable, and with less progesterone to regulate growth of the endometrium, the uterine lining may become thicker before it sheds. This can result in heavier periods.
Fibroids
and endometriosis, both of which are fueled by estrogen, might become more troublesome too.
Hot Flashes and Night Sweats:

An estimated 30-35% of perimenopausal and menopausal people suffer sudden waves of body heat with flushing and sweating that can last 5-10 minutes. These episodes can occur at night while sleeping too.
Sleep Disturbances:

About 40% of perimenopausal people have sleep problems – trouble falling and staying asleep. This can lead to insomnia and fractured sleep problems and, of course, contribute to fatigue and fogginess.
Vaginal Changes:

During late perimenopause, falling estrogen levels can cause vaginal tissue to become thinner, drier, and less elastic, which can lead to itching, irritation, painful sexual intercourse, and an increase in
urinary tract infections (UTIs).
Mood Symptoms:

Hormonal fluctuations during perimenopause might contribute to mood changes, including irritability, anxiety, and depression. That said, studies haven’t proven a definitive correlation between hormone levels and new mood symptoms. Of course, the unpredictability of perimenopause can be stressful and provoke irritability on its own. The best predictors of mood symptoms, in general, are life stress, poor overall health, and personal and family history.
Fatigue:
Some people experience increased fatigue and lack of energy during perimenopause.
Changes in Libido:
Some people report lower desire and more difficulty being aroused.
Memory and Concentration Issues:
Perimenopause can affect memory, concentration, and cognitive function.
Joint and Muscle Pain:
Perimenopause can be associated with joint and muscle pain.
Seeking medical treatment for your menopause symtpoms? Join Tia and schedule an appointment at the clinic nearest you.
Located in New York, Phoenix, San Francisco, and Los Angeles.

editorialText.title: Are there ways to treat these symptoms?

children.text: The short answer: Yes. There are several useful treatment strategies for managing perimenopause and menopause symptoms, including non-prescription remedies, medical interventions, and lifestyle changes. As always, it’s essential to discuss new, bothersome, or worsening symptoms with a healthcare provider to determine the best approach.
Hot Flashes:

The first rule of thumb is to avoid possible triggers (you’ll know them), like warm air temperatures, hot beverages, and spicy foods. For example, dressing in layers so you can quickly remove layers is always a good idea. Some people find relief with certain medications, like SSRI antidepressants, gabapentin, and clonidine, but there are side effects that need to be considered. For severe hot flashes, estrogen replacement is the only true fix.
Irregular Periods:
Low-dose
birth control

can be a good choice here. By suppressing ovulation, these meds modulate menstrual flow, regulate periods, and stabilize endometriosis. They also protect against endometrial and ovarian cancer, stave off hot flashes, reduce vaginal dryness, and prevent bone loss.
Vaginal Dryness:

Low-dose contraceptives or vaginal estrogen therapy can help relieve this symptom. But there are other approaches. Vaginal moisturizers can improve moisture and elasticity and lubricants (find a good water-based lube) can make intercourse less painful. Additionally, sexual activity seems to improve vaginal tone and help maintain a healthy acidic environment that can be protective against infections.
Non-prescription Remedies:
Some people find relief using products like soy-based supplements, black cohosh, and herbal remedies, but efficacy varies. Definitely discuss their usage with a care provider.
Bone Health:
Maintaining bone health is a crucial consideration during menopause and calcium and Vitamin D are two supplements often recommended to reduce the risk of osteoporosis.

editorialText.title: What lifestyle changes help with symptoms?

children.text: Adopting certain lifestyle changes can help manage symptoms of perimenopause and menopause and contribute to overall health and wellbeing. Here are some lifestyle habits that might beneficial:
Regular exercise:
Both aerobic exercises and strength training helps manage weight, boost mood, and improve sleep.
Healthy Diet:
Consuming a well-balanced diet rich in fresh fruits and vegetables, whole grains, healthy oils, and lean proteins is never a bad move.
Stress Management:
Practicing stress-reducing techniques, like meditation, yoga, deep breathing, and mindfulness can have positive impacts on mood and wellbeing.
Adequate Sleep:
Thoughtful sleep hygiene – consistent sleep schedule, comfortable sleep environment (dark and cool), relaxing before bedtime – might help alleviate symptoms.
Reduce Caffeine and Alcohol:
They can contribute to poor sleep and exacerbate hot flashes.
Stay Hydrated:
A good habit for overall health.
Maintain a Healthy Weight:
Good eating habits + regular exercise. A healthy weight can alleviate symptoms and reduce the risk of health conditions.

A reminder: Treatment decisions should be personalized based on your own health history, personal preferences, and subjective symptomology. Regular check-up and check-ins with a provider are essential for monitoring progress and adjusting the plan.

editorialText.title: Take control of your own menopausal journey

children.text: Perimenopause and menopause are natural biological processes that all women face, usually beginning in their forties, that cause numerous symptoms related to declining supplies of estrogen and progesterone. Hot flashes tend to get the most buzz, but other symptoms (vaginal dryness, mood changes, sleep disturbances) can be just as disruptive and discomforting. Thankfully, there are many ways to manage these symptoms and many more lifestyle adjustments that can lighten the emotional and physical load of perimenopause and menopause as well. And while several important studies have demonstrated an association between menopausal symptoms and lower quality of life, many menopausal women report positive aspects associated with this life stage as well. So, we leave you with a few good reasons to have hope:

No more periods or menstrual symptoms. Sex without worrying about pregnancy. Disappearing body hair. Shrinking uterine fibroids. A renewed zest for life. More confidence. Increased bonding with other women.
If you are experiencing symptoms of perimenopause or menopause,
sign up for a Tia membership
and schedule an appointment with the
clinic nearest you

. Tia's clinics are located in New York, Arizona, Los Angeles, and San Francisco. Let Tia’s team of sexual and reproductive health experts address your concerns with the highest quality, most comfortable gynecological care around.

children.text: Symptoms we can help with
Cramps Or Pelvic Pain

Cramps or pelvic pain can be uncomfortable, disruptive, and hard to ignore. A visit for these symptoms helps uncover what’s going on, whether it’s cycle-related changes, muscle tension, or an underlying condition, and creates a personalized plan to relieve pain and help you feel more in control of your body.
Abnormal Bleeding

From PMS to PCOS, heavy periods to no periods, we can help you evaluate and manage your cycle. Heavy bleeding, clots, and spotting can be symptoms of an underlying issue, and they are never something you have to just "deal with." We'll work together to diagnose what's causing the abnormal bleeding and find a treatment for you. Stop worrying about your cycle and start working toward a healthier, more predictable flow.
Vaginal Infection

From chronic yeast infections to Bacterial Vaginosis (BV) to that "not-sure-what-but-something-is-up" feeling, we've got you covered with diagnosis, treatment, and prevention. We know these symptoms are frustrating, often recurrent, and rarely discussed in a supportive setting. At Tia, we provide expert care to find the root cause, address imbalances, and create a personalized plan to get you back to feeling comfortable and confident, without judgment.

modules.title: Where you can get dedicated care

children.text: Integrated women’s health, all in one place
The Problem

Healthcare should be a relationship, not a series of disconnected transactions. But all too often, it's not. Gynecological and sexual health is too often siloed, forcing you to go to separate providers for your annual exam, a painful sex consult, or a new birth control prescription. Many women feel isolated and alone, passed from one specialist to the next without a central provider who truly knows their entire health story. We know these frustrations are real. In a 2022 report, nearly
one in three women said they felt dismissed by their doctor.

Tia’s Solution

We believe women deserve care that treats the whole person. Our model ensures your gynecology issues are never treated in isolation. Our providers are trained to connect the dots between symptoms like irregular periods or painful sex and your overall physical, mental, and emotional health. This allows us to offer holistic, trauma-informed care that gets to the root cause of conditions like painful sex, irregular bleeding, chronic pelvic pain . These symptoms often connect back to hormones or stress. Our providers don’t just prescribe birth control; we help you find the option that best fits your body, values, and lifestyle.
This approach helps women get comprehensive care, covering all aspects of their well-being:
Integrated Prevention:
After joining Tia, members complete
life-saving screenings at rates 2–3x the national average
, ensuring your gynecologic health (like Pap tests and cervical cancer screenings) is proactively managed.
Seamless Specialty Referrals:
If you need specialty care, Tia partners with top health systems to coordinate your referral while remaining your primary care home.
Mental Health is GYN Health
88.2% of Tia patients were screened for depression
because we recognize the deep connection between chronic pain, stress, and mental health.

Why Gynecological and Sexual Health matters for women
Your body is a connected system.

Gynecological symptoms aren't isolated; they’re often signals from the rest of your body. A dedicated care team helps connect the dots, understanding how stress, sleep, and overall health can influence things like your period or pelvic pain, sexual wellness, or irregular bleeding.
Your symptoms deserve to be taken seriously.

You're not imagining it. For too long, women's pain—from endometriosis to chronic pelvic issues—has been dismissed and minimized. You deserve care from providers trained to understand the full picture of women's reproductive and sexual health who will listen actively and never brush off your concerns
Prevention is the foundation of lifelong health.

Building a relationship with a trusted primary care team means catching problems early, from getting your cervical cancer screening (Pap test) on time to addressing changes in your reproductive health , ensuring you stay healthier for the long term.

"There is no question too personal, too complicated, or too embarrassing when it comes to your gynecologic and sexual health. Our primary role is to be a compassionate, non-judgmental partner in your total well-being."
Rebecca Reilly, DNP, FNP-C
, Market Medical Director

testimonials.Content: I had my IUD removed and replaced today which is probably one of the most uncomfortable procedures EVER but the care team made sure that my comfort was prioritized. My NP was extremely thorough and asked if I had any questions and walked me through every step as it was happening. I felt very taken care of! testimonials.Content: I’ve read many reviews about this facility, but my experience was truly exceptional. I’ve had a lot of trauma around women’s health and avoided gynecologic care for years, but Tia completely changed that. Dr. Ryan Darvish is the first provider who made me feel genuinely safe and cared for. After an abnormal pap, I went through several difficult procedures, and he guided me through each one with compassion, clear communication, and a focus on keeping me comfortable. The same care and support were present during my IUD removal and replacement, which he made nearly painless. I’ve never felt so at ease during appointments that once felt overwhelming. Dr. Darvish is truly a gift, and I recommend him to anyone with fear or trauma around gynecologic care. Tia is now the only place I’ll go for my health needs.
testimonials.Title: Emily F., Tia Patient

testimonials.Content: I cannot say enough good things about my experience at Tia Clinic in Studio City, thanks to Jamee and Alauna. Their customer service was truly unmatched—every interaction was thoughtful, kind, and professional. modules.title: Perimenopause & menopause care for the whole you
Where you can get dedicated care

children.text: The Care Gap in Menopause
Menopause is a universal experience, yet most women are still left to navigate it alone. Up to
80% of women experience disruptive symptoms
such as hot flashes, sleep changes, mood shifts, or brain fog.
Despite how common this transition is, care for this life stage remains underresearched and underserved. Too often, women find themselves:
Told to "Wait it Out":
Being dismissed or told their symptoms are just "part of aging".
Misdiagnosed with Other Conditions:
Symptoms like anxiety or depression are treated without addressing the root hormonal cause.
Bounced Between Specialists:
Having to piece together disjointed care from multiple doctors who only treat one symptom at a time.
Uninformed:
In a
2023 U.S. survey
, only one in four women said they felt very knowledgeable about menopause, and two-thirds said more doctors trained in menopause care are needed.
This gap leaves millions of women struggling in silence, leading to years of preventable discomfort and missed opportunities to protect long-term health.
Takes 3–5 minutes to create an account • No membership fee to book • Insurance accepted
Do These Symptoms Sound Familiar?
Tia’s perimenopause & menopause care may be right for you if you’re experiencing:
Hot flashes or night sweats that disrupt your sleep.
Unpredictable cycle changes (heavy, light, or skipped periods).
Mood swings, anxiety, irritability, or feeling unlike yourself.
Brain fog, poor concentration, or other cognitive shifts.
New or worsening skin changes (acne, dryness, or texture).
Weight fluctuation you can't explain.
Uncertainty about whether you're in perimenopause (age 30s-50s).
The need for clear guidance on HRT or non-hormone options.
The desire for a provider who treats the whole-body impact of this life stage.
A need for ongoing support for your mental health and long-term wellness.
Takes 3–5 minutes to create an account • No membership fee to book • Insurance accepted
How Tia is different with Perimenopause & Menopause

Menopause is a whole-body transition, and we think your care should be, too. We don't believe in fragmented care that treats one symptom in isolation. Tia is built to be your trusted women's health home, providing comprehensive support right where you are.
Here's how Tia is different:
Whole-Health Care, Not Just Menopause

We address the full range of your symptoms—from hot flashes to mood and sleep—through a single, connected approach. Because Tia is your primary care home, we care for your whole health, including other conditions like diabetes or high blood pressure.
A Personalized, Evidence-Based Plan

Your care plan is tailored to you, not one-size-fits-all. We back your choices with evidence-based options, combining Menopausal Hormonal Therapy (MHT) with integrative interventions like acupuncture, nutrition, and mental health support from your care team.
Ongoing, Connected Care

You’re supported by a collaborative team of menopause-trained providers who stay with you through every phase of this journey. With continuous follow-ups and treatment management, your care remains connected, effective, and built for the long term.
Why care for perimenopause & menopause matters

"We listen to you first. We'll use various assessments and tests to get to the bottom of your symptoms we take you seriously. This isn't a disease, this is a sacred time and by focusing on your quality of life we can get you feeling like yourself again".
Lara Virginia Baldwin, WHNP, CNM
Our whole-body, integrative approach to perimenopause & menopause care
✓ HRT, Prescriptions, Labs, & Supplements
Shared decision-making and expert guidance on hormonal and non-hormonal options tailored to you
✓ Acupuncture, Nutrition & Lifestyle Guidance
Clinically informed holistic tools in your care plan to support sleep, mood, and symptom relief
✓ Education & Follow Ups
Ongoing visits, expert guidance to adjust your personalized care plan as your symptoms and life evolve
✓ Mental Health Support
Care that addresses the mood changes and anxiety that aren't "just in your head"
Takes 3–5 minutes to create an account • No membership fee to book • Insurance accepted
Book an appointment

Every stage of perimenopause and menopause deserves thoughtful, continuous care. Connect with Tia to get the support you need to feel balanced, confident, and well through every change your body goes through.

children.text: What is a skin care appointment?
An acne, rash, or skin change visit at Tia can be facilitated in clinic or virtually and is conducted by an MD or nurse pracitioner.
When should you schedule a skin care appointment?
It is recommended to schedule an appointment to discuss skin changes if:
Your symptoms are not responding to over the counter treatment.
Your skin changes are associated with the menstrual cycle.
Your symptoms are becoming bothersome or are worsening over time.
What to expect during a skin care appointment
Here’s what you can expect during a Tia dermatology appointment:
A comprehensive assessment:
Your provider will factor in your skin concerns, overall health, and well-being to create your personalized treatment plan.
A customized Care Plan:

Based on your assessment, your provider will work with you to develop a personalized treatment plan. This may include a combination of medications, lifestyle recommendations, holistic therapies like acupuncture, or other interventions aimed at optimizing your skin health.
Follow-up care:
Seeing changes in your skin can take time, and our team will be there every step of the way to make sure your Care Plan is working.

After your appointment, we'll provide clear guidance on next steps. Whether it's booking a follow-up, adjusting your treatment plan, or providing ongoing support, we'll ensure you get the comprehensive care you deserve.
Emotional support:

Skin concerns can bring up a lot of feelings, and we're here to listen, provide support, and put you at ease. At Tia, care is collaborative. You’re always welcome to ask questions, express concerns, or discuss any care preferences with your provider.
Potential treatment plans for skin issues
Once your provider has conducted a thorough medical exam and reviewed your medical history associated with your skin issues, there are several treatment options they might recommend:
Depending on your symptoms, your provider might recommend additional lab testing.
Your provider might recommened keeping a symptom journal to track when, how often, and for how long your symptoms appear.
Depending on symptoms and severity, ertain over the counter medication can be recommended or your provider will perscribe medication.
If your provider suspects that your symptoms are more severe, you might recieve a referral to a dermatology specialist.
Got skin issues? Join Tia and schedule an appointment virtualy or at the clinic nearest you!
Located in New York, Scottsdale, San Francisco, and Los Angeles.

children.text: Integrated women’s health, all in one place
The Problem

Healthcare should be a relationship, not a series of disconnected transactions. But all too often, it's not. Many women feel isolated and alone, passed from one specialist to the next without a central provider who truly knows their entire health story.
We know these frustrations are real. In a 2022 report, nearly one in three women said they felt dismissed by their doctor and that number climbs to 46% among women ages 18-35,
reporting a negative interaction with a healthcare provider
. This can lead to missed connections between symptoms, overlooked diagnoses, and the frustrating feeling that no one is truly listening.

We believe women deserve care that treats the whole person. Our primary care gives women one connected place for their whole health,from a cold to chronic conditions. Our providers take time to understand your physical, mental, and emotional health to create a personalized plan built around you.
Our approach helps women live healthier, more vibrant lives:

Why Primary Care matters for women
Your body is a connected system.

Women's health isn't just about reproductive organs; it’s about the whole person. A dedicated primary care team helps connect the dots across your health, offering continuous care to understand your full health story.
Prevention is the foundation of lifelong health

Building a relationship with a primary care team means catching problems early, managing chronic conditions effectively, and staying healthier long term, instead of just reacting when something feels wrong.
Women’s health deserves specialized care

You’re not imagining it. Women are more susceptible to conditions like migraines and osteoporosis, and hormones influence everything from mood to skin and gut health. Yet, women’s symptoms are often minimized and misdiagnosed. You deserve care from providers trained to understand the whole picture of women's biology and will take your symptoms seriously.

“All women need a home base for their health. So much of traditional healthcare is fragmented, treating each symptom in isolation. At Tia, our providers are trained to connect the dots across your physical, mental, and emotional health. We believe in taking the time to listen your unique story because we know your health isn't a series of disconnected issues—it's a whole picture. Our goal is to empower you with the knowledge to manage your health proactively, helping you not just get well, but stay well."
Emily Rymland, DNP, FNP
, Market Medical Director
How Tia is different for primary care
✓ Hybrid care, built for modern women
In-person and virtual care, so you can choose what works for you. Visit our clinics for hands-on, welcoming care, or schedule virtual visits for quick check-ups or prescription refills from home.
✓ One record, one plan, one team
Every provider you see at Tia works together from a single health record and shared Care Plan, so your care stays connected, not fragmented.
✓ Holistic and comprehensive care
We treat your health as one connected system — integrating mind, body, and hormones to uncover the “why” behind your symptoms and help you feel well from every angle.
✓ Care that grows with you
No need to switch practices as your needs change. Your team supports you though every life stage and health need.

children.text: Symptoms we can help with
Cramps Or Pelvic Pain

Cramps or pelvic pain can be uncomfortable, disruptive, and hard to ignore. A visit for these symptoms helps uncover what’s going on, whether it’s cycle-related changes, muscle tension, or an underlying condition, and creates a personalized plan to relieve pain and help you feel more in control of your body.
Abnormal Bleeding

From PMS to PCOS, heavy periods to no periods, we can help you evaluate and manage your cycle. Heavy bleeding, clots, and spotting can be symptoms of an underlying issue, and they are never something you have to just "deal with." We'll work together to diagnose what's causing the abnormal bleeding and find a treatment for you. Stop worrying about your cycle and start working toward a healthier, more predictable flow.
Vaginal Infection

From chronic yeast infections to Bacterial Vaginosis (BV) to that "not-sure-what-but-something-is-up" feeling, we've got you covered with diagnosis, treatment, and prevention. We know these symptoms are frustrating, often recurrent, and rarely discussed in a supportive setting. At Tia, we provide expert care to find the root cause, address imbalances, and create a personalized plan to get you back to feeling comfortable and confident, without judgment.

Cold or Flu Like Symptoms

Ear Issues

Experiencing ear pain, pressure, ringing, or changes in hearing? A visit for ear issues helps uncover what’s causing your symptoms and provides a personalized plan to relieve discomfort, restore balance, and help you feel like yourself again.
Sleep Disturbances

Stomach Pain & Issues

children.text: Integrated women’s health, all in one place
The Problem

Healthcare should be a relationship, not a series of disconnected transactions. But all too often, it's not. Many women feel isolated and alone, passed from one specialist to the next without a central provider who truly knows their entire health story.
We know these frustrations are real. In a 2022 report, nearly one in three women said they felt dismissed by their doctor and that number climbs to 46% among women ages 18-35,
reporting a negative interaction with a healthcare provider
. This can lead to missed connections between symptoms, overlooked diagnoses, and the frustrating feeling that no one is truly listening.

Tia’s Solution

We believe women deserve care that treats the whole person. Our primary care gives women one connected place for their whole health,from a cold to chronic conditions. Our providers take time to understand your physical, mental, and emotional health to create a personalized plan built around you.
Our approach helps women live healthier, more vibrant lives:
70% of Tia’s members hadn’t had a preventative exam in the year before joining Tia. After joining,
they complete life-saving screenings at rates 2-3x the national average.
88.2% of Tia patients were screened for depression, nearly
double the national benchmark
because we see mental health as a core part of your health.
It's healthcare that's proactive, not just reactive, designed to help you get well and stay well for the long term.

Why Primary Care matters for women
Your body is a connected system.

Women's health isn't just about reproductive organs; it’s about the whole person. A dedicated primary care team helps connect the dots across your health, offering continuous care to understand your full health story.
Prevention is the foundation of lifelong health

Building a relationship with a primary care team means catching problems early, managing chronic conditions effectively, and staying healthier long term, instead of just reacting when something feels wrong.
Women’s health deserves specialized care

You’re not imagining it. Women are more susceptible to conditions like migraines and osteoporosis, and hormones influence everything from mood to skin and gut health. Yet, women’s symptoms are often minimized and misdiagnosed. You deserve care from providers trained to understand the whole picture of women's biology and will take your symptoms seriously.

“All women need a home base for their health. So much of traditional healthcare is fragmented, treating each symptom in isolation. At Tia, our providers are trained to connect the dots across your physical, mental, and emotional health. We believe in taking the time to listen your unique story because we know your health isn't a series of disconnected issues—it's a whole picture. Our goal is to empower you with the knowledge to manage your health proactively, helping you not just get well, but stay well."
Emily Rymland, DNP, FNP
, Market Medical Director
How Tia is different for primary care
✓ Hybrid care, built for modern women
In-person and virtual care, so you can choose what works for you. Visit our clinics for hands-on, welcoming care, or schedule virtual visits for quick check-ups or prescription refills from home.
✓ One record, one plan, one team
Every provider you see at Tia works together from a single health record and shared Care Plan, so your care stays connected, not fragmented.
✓ Holistic and comprehensive care
We treat your health as one connected system — integrating mind, body, and hormones to uncover the “why” behind your symptoms and help you feel well from every angle.
✓ Care that grows with you
No need to switch practices as your needs change. Your team supports you though every life stage and health need.

children.text: Let's refresh - What is a pap smear?
During a Pap smear, a sample of cervical cells is collected and examined under a microscope.
An abnormal result

indicates that there are changes in the size, shape, or appearance of the cervical cells, which could be indicative of infections, inflammation, precancerous, or cancerous conditions. Further diagnostic tests or procedures may be recommended based on the degree and nature of the abnormalities to determine the appropriate course of action for the individual's health. Regular Pap smears are essential for early detection and prevention of cervical cancer.
Did you know?

About 5% of all Pap tests will be abnormal, meaning that the sample contains atypical cervical cells. However, the majority of these cells are not cancerous or even precancerous. An abnormal Pap test result does not mean cancer, but it does require follow-up to rule out the possibility of cancer.
When to schedule an appointment regarding an abnormal pap smear result
This visit type might be recommended if:
You have a history of abnormal pap smears and want to talk about screening recommendations.
Your
pap results
at Tia are abnormal and you want to discuss further.
You have completed abnormal pap follow-up (colposcopy, LEEP) and want to discuss next steps and screening recommendations.
What to expect during your appointment

This is a consultation based visit to review previous pap smear results and discuss recommendations. It is very helpful if previous results are available and that they be uploaded to your Tia provider ahead of this visit.
Potential treatment plans and next steps
Once your provider has reviewed your
previous pap smear results

and discussed your concerns and questions, your provider will provide potential updated cervical cancer screening recommendations which may include future pap recommendations, an appointment for a pap smear or a colposcopy. In some cases, this may require referral to a gynecological surgical specialist.
Did you know?
Abnormal Pap tests are very common. In fact, of the 3 million women with abnormal Pap tests each year, less than 1% (13,240 cases) will be diagnosed with cervical cancer.
Questions about your pap test? Join Tia's membership and schedule an appointment to discuss your options at the clinic nearest you!
Located in New York, Scottsdale, San Francisco, and Los Angeles.

children.text: Symptoms we can help with
Cramps Or Pelvic Pain

Cramps or pelvic pain can be uncomfortable, disruptive, and hard to ignore. A visit for these symptoms helps uncover what’s going on, whether it’s cycle-related changes, muscle tension, or an underlying condition, and creates a personalized plan to relieve pain and help you feel more in control of your body.
Abnormal Bleeding

From PMS to PCOS, heavy periods to no periods, we can help you evaluate and manage your cycle. Heavy bleeding, clots, and spotting can be symptoms of an underlying issue, and they are never something you have to just "deal with." We'll work together to diagnose what's causing the abnormal bleeding and find a treatment for you. Stop worrying about your cycle and start working toward a healthier, more predictable flow.
Vaginal Infection

From chronic yeast infections to Bacterial Vaginosis (BV) to that "not-sure-what-but-something-is-up" feeling, we've got you covered with diagnosis, treatment, and prevention. We know these symptoms are frustrating, often recurrent, and rarely discussed in a supportive setting. At Tia, we provide expert care to find the root cause, address imbalances, and create a personalized plan to get you back to feeling comfortable and confident, without judgment.

Healthcare should be a relationship, not a series of disconnected transactions. But all too often, it's not. Gynecological and sexual health is too often siloed, forcing you to go to separate providers for your annual exam, a painful sex consult, or a new birth control prescription. Why Gynecological and Sexual Health matters for women
Your body is a connected system.

Gynecological symptoms aren't isolated; they’re often signals from the rest of your body. A dedicated care team helps connect the dots, understanding how stress, sleep, and overall health can influence things like your period or pelvic pain, sexual wellness, or irregular bleeding.
Your symptoms deserve to be taken seriously.

You're not imagining it. For too long, women's pain—from endometriosis to chronic pelvic issues—has been dismissed and minimized. You deserve care from providers trained to understand the full picture of women's reproductive and sexual health who will listen actively and never brush off your concerns
Prevention is the foundation of lifelong health.

Building a relationship with a trusted primary care team means catching problems early, from getting your cervical cancer screening (Pap test) on time to addressing changes in your reproductive health , ensuring you stay healthier for the long term.

"There is no question too personal, too complicated, or too embarrassing when it comes to your gynecologic and sexual health. Our primary role is to be a compassionate, non-judgmental partner in your total well-being."
Rebecca Reilly, DNP, FNP-C
, Market Medical Director
How Tia is different for Sexual and Gynecological health
✓ Hybrid care, built for modern women
Seamlessly transition between virtual consults for birth control and in-person procedures like IUD insertion.
✓ One record, one plan, one team
Your primary care provider is always in sync with your GYN care, so you never have to repeat your history to get a Pap test, STI screen, or tackle a more complex condition.
✓ Seamless Specialty Access
Tia partners with leading health systems to ensure that if you need specialty care—for complex issues like advanced endometriosis—you are referred and supported while Tia remains your home base.
✓ Holistic/comprehensive care
We use both medication and integrative options (like acupuncture or pelvic floor therapy referrals) for chronic conditions like PCOS or painful sex, aiming to get to the root and give you options.
✓ Care that grows with you
We are your health home for every stage—from your first Pap test to navigating menopause and beyond.

children.text: PCOS Treatment

items: Whole-person care for managing symptoms of Polycystic Ovarian Syndrome (PCOS)
Care that looks at the root cause, from hormonal imbalances to insulin resistance
Personalized plans based on your unique goals, including if and when you want to conceive
Ongoing, integrated support for a chronic condition, with care that sees you as a whole person—not just a diagnosis

children.text: Takes 3–5 minutes to create an account • No membership fee to book • Insurance accepted
Already a Tia member? Book here
PCOS Consultation: What to Expect?
Comprehensive PCOS diagnosis and assessment

Your journey begins with a comprehensive assessment to discuss your current symptoms, medical history, and family risk factors. We take a deep dive into your period history and pregnancy goals to build a complete picture of your health. This visit may also result in orders for bloodwork or an ultrasound to confirm a diagnosis.
Building your personalized PCOS treatment plan

Together, we'll create a personalized care plan to manage your specific symptoms. You and your provider will determine the best path for you based on your symptoms, overall health, and personal goals. We’ll explore all of your options, including lifestyle changes, supplements, and medication.
Continuing support for long-term PCOS management

PCOS is a chronic condition, and we’re here for the long term. Your Care Team provides continuous, relationship-based support to track your progress, adjust your plan as needed, and ensure your care is comprehensive, not piecemeal.
Who should have a PCOS appointment?
This visit is for anyone with a PCOS diagnosis or symptoms that may point to PCOS.
Symptoms we care for:
Irregular, long, or absent periods
Excess facial or body hair growth
Acne
Weight gain or difficulty losing weight
Difficulty getting pregnant
How the PCOS appointment can help
This care is designed to empower you with a compassionate and comprehensive plan to manage your symptoms and improve your quality of life.
This assessment helps you:
Understand your body better
through a comprehensive evaluation of your hormonal and metabolic health.
Explore all your options
in a judgment-free space, from lifestyle changes to medication.
Create a personalized care plan
tailored to your unique symptoms and goals.
Manage your PCOS symptoms effectively
with a team that supports you without shame or blame.
Insurance and Pricing
Is this covered by insurance?
Yes! Tia will bill your insurance. Just like at any doctor’s office, you may be responsible for a co-pay or coinsurance, depending on your health plan.
Potential treatment plans for PCOS

There is no cure for PCOS but there are many options to manage your symptoms. Your provider will determine the best treatment for you based on your symptoms, medical history, and overall health, as well as your desire to conceive. Treatment options may include medication, lifestyle changes, or a combination of both.

cells: This medication suppresses androgens which can improve the symptoms of unwanted hair growth and acne.
This diabetes drug is believed to work by improving insulin sensitivity, which is commonly associated with PCOS. By lowering your insulin level, metformin allows many women to cycle more normally.

For those who do not want to become pregnant, treating PCOS often involves taking hormonal birth control to manage the symptoms of PCOS, like painful periods, acne, and excess body hair. The pill, the patch, and the ring work by preventing ovulation which reduces the number of cysts on the ovary.
Is thought to improve insulin sensitivity, lower blood pressure and decrease inflammation.
Inositol may help reduce blood triglyceride levels, improve insulin function, lower blood pressure and promote ovulation in women with PCOS.
Vitamin D promotes insulin sensitivity in addition to helping with calcium absorption.
Zinc may improve insulin resistance, decrease cramping with periods, unwanted hair growth and acne.
This is a phytonutrient derived from vegetables that blocks androgen receptors which can help with acne and unwanted hair growth.
This has significant anti-androgen effects and contains compounds called polyphenols that have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antibacterial properties.

The peony and licorice (Shao Yao Gan Cao Tang) combination has undergone clinical trials in which it was found to reduce testosterone and improve cycle regularity. Caution with high blood pressure as licorice may raise blood pressure.
Aim to get at least 25 grams of fiber per day.
Healthy fats can help sustain your energy and support healthy cholesterol levels.
Try more restorative forms of exercise rather than high intensity.
Acupuncture can help to regulate the cycle, encourage healthy ovulation, reduce signs of androgen excess, and support metabolism.

This care is designed to empower you with a compassionate and comprehensive plan to manage your symptoms and improve your quality of life.
This assessment helps you:
Understand your body better
through a comprehensive evaluation of your hormonal and metabolic health.
Explore all your options
in a judgment-free space, from lifestyle changes to medication.
Create a personalized care plan
tailored to your unique symptoms and goals.
Manage your PCOS symptoms effectively
with a team that supports you without shame or blame.
Insurance and Pricing
Is this covered by insurance?
Yes! Tia will bill your insurance. Just like at any doctor’s office, you may be responsible for a co-pay or coinsurance, depending on your health plan.
Potential treatment plans for PCOS

There is no cure for PCOS but there are many options to manage your symptoms. Your provider will determine the best treatment for you based on your symptoms, medical history, and overall health, as well as your desire to conceive. Treatment options may include medication, lifestyle changes, or a combination of both.

cells: This medication suppresses androgens which can improve the symptoms of unwanted hair growth and acne.
This diabetes drug is believed to work by improving insulin sensitivity, which is commonly associated with PCOS. By lowering your insulin level, metformin allows many women to cycle more normally.

For those who do not want to become pregnant, treating PCOS often involves taking hormonal birth control to manage the symptoms of PCOS, like painful periods, acne, and excess body hair. The pill, the patch, and the ring work by preventing ovulation which reduces the number of cysts on the ovary.
Is thought to improve insulin sensitivity, lower blood pressure and decrease inflammation.
Inositol may help reduce blood triglyceride levels, improve insulin function, lower blood pressure and promote ovulation in women with PCOS.
Vitamin D promotes insulin sensitivity in addition to helping with calcium absorption.
Zinc may improve insulin resistance, decrease cramping with periods, unwanted hair growth and acne.
This is a phytonutrient derived from vegetables that blocks androgen receptors which can help with acne and unwanted hair growth.
This has significant anti-androgen effects and contains compounds called polyphenols that have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antibacterial properties.

The peony and licorice (Shao Yao Gan Cao Tang) combination has undergone clinical trials in which it was found to reduce testosterone and improve cycle regularity. Caution with high blood pressure as licorice may raise blood pressure.
Aim to get at least 25 grams of fiber per day.
Healthy fats can help sustain your energy and support healthy cholesterol levels.
Try more restorative forms of exercise rather than high intensity.
Acupuncture can help to regulate the cycle, encourage healthy ovulation, reduce signs of androgen excess, and support metabolism.
