children.text: Your first visit is a comprehensive evaluation where you'll discuss your symptoms, health history, and goals with a provider. The visit may result in orders for additional testing like bloodwork or an ultrasound to help make a diagnosis and create your care plan.

faqs.question: What happens at my first PCOS visit?

children.text: Not at all. Hormonal birth control is an effective option for managing symptoms if you do not want to become pregnant. However, your provider will work with you to find the best treatment based on your personal goals, which can include non-hormonal medications, supplements, and lifestyle changes.

faqs.question: Do I have to take birth control to manage PCOS?

children.text: While there is no cure for PCOS, there are many effective options to manage your symptoms and reduce your risk for other health concerns like diabetes. Our goal is to create a sustainable, long-term plan that helps you feel your best.

faqs.question: Is PCOS curable?

children.text: We treat the whole you, not just a single symptom. Our integrative model combines primary care, gynecology, and even acupuncture to address the root hormonal imbalances of PCOS. We create a truly personalized plan that evolves with you over time.

faqs.question: What makes Tia’s approach to PCOS care different?

children.text: Yes, a PCOS evaluation can take place either in-clinic or virtually. Your provider will help you determine the best setting for your follow-up care

faqs.question: Can I get this care virtually?

children.text: Polycystic Ovary Syndrome (PCOS) is a common, if complex, hormonal disorder affecting people with ovaries, primarily during their reproductive years, that can cause infertility and impact overall physical and emotional well-being. Characterized by a constellation of symptoms, including irregular or absent ovulation, PCOS is a global public health concern that affects 5-15% of women and people assigned female at birth (AFAB). Strikingly, according to the best available estimates,
50-70% of women with PCOS remain undiagnosed
—and suffer unnecessarily because of it.

Hopefully these numbers get your attention. Many people are struggling with PCOS—including struggling to get pregnant and get answers—and the social stigma its diagnosis often carries. But if you learn one thing from this article, let it be this: while PCOS is one of the leading causes of infertility and isn’t curable, it
is
manageable and treatable. You
can
get pregnant if you have PCOS. You
can
have a healthy pregnancy. And you
can
deliver a healthy baby.
At
Tia

, our team of collaborative care experts understands the unique needs of people with PCOS and the importance of personalized care that empowers women to navigate their fertility journeys on their terms and without pressure, fear, or judgment. Keep reading to learn more about PCOS and Tia’s holistic, personalized approach to this notoriously complicated condition.

editorialText.title: Understanding PCOS and its effects on pregnancy

children.text: PCOS is a common endocrine disorder that disrupts the delicate balance of hormones involved in ovulation and menstruation, and it manifests with a combination of symptoms related to these hormonal imbalances. PCOS is characterized by irregular or absent menstrual cycles, elevated androgen levels (a male sex hormone present in small amounts in women), and the presence of numerous small cysts on the ovaries. Despite its name, not all women with PCOS develop ovarian cysts. And, confusingly, some women with ovarian cysts don’t meet the diagnostic criteria for PCOS.

While the exact cause of PCOS remains unclear, it likely involves a combination of genetic and environmental factors, including insulin resistance and inflammation. Importantly, women with a family history of PCOS or type 2 diabetes seem to be at a higher risk for developing PCOS.

One hallmark feature of PCOS is its significant impact on fertility. The hormonal imbalances seen in PCOS often disrupt the normal ovulatory process, which leads to irregular or absent ovulation. Since ovulation is the process that releases a mature egg from the ovaries and makes it available for fertilization by sperm, it’s easy to understand why unpredictable or absent ovulation makes pregnancy difficult, if not impossible, to achieve without proper counseling and treatment.
Besides infertility, PCOS can present with several other symptoms:
Irregular menstrual cycles
. Women with PCOS might have missed, unpredictable, or absent periods—though it’s not uncommon for them to experience heavy and long periods as well.
Hirsutism
. Excessive hair on the face, chin, chest, back, or parts of the body where men usually have hair is often seen in people with PCOS.
Acne and/or male-pattern baldness.
Hirsutism, acne, and male-pattern baldness are most likely due to excess androgen levels that often accompany PCOS.
Weight gain,
especially around the belly.
Polycystic ovaries
that might feel large on exam or appear large on imaging.
Skin tags
or small pieces of excess skin on the neck or armpits.
Dark or thick patches of skin
particularly along neck creases,
in the groin area, in the armpits, or underneath the breasts.
Experiencing PCOS symptoms? Sign up for Tia's membership and schedule an appointment today.
With appropriate medical management aimed at reducing symptoms and
improving ovulation, women with PCOS
can
get pregnant—but they may face certain challenges during pregnancy that require careful monitoring and possible medical intervention.
Some of the potential complications associated with pregnancy in women with PCOS:
Gestational diabetes.

People with PCOS have an increased risk of developing gestational diabetes. While poorly understood, researchers postulate that fluctuating hormones caused by pregnancy make it more difficult for the body to process blood sugar effectively.
Preeclampisa

. There’s a strong association between PCOS and preeclampsia, a potentially dangerous condition characterized by high blood pressure and proteins in the urine, which can cause organ damage. Regular prenatal visits and routine monitoring are crucial to protect both the parent and baby and prevent preeclampsia from developing into a life-threatening condition called eclampsia.
Preterm birth.
There is an increased risk of preterm birth in women with PCOS.
Miscarriage.
People with PCOS have a slightly higher risk of miscarriage compared to those without the condition.
Increased C-section rates.
Those diagnosed with PCOS have a higher likelihood of delivering via Cesarean section, often due to complications related to gestational diabetes, preeclampsia, and fetal distress.
Neonatal complications.
Babies whose parents have PCOS often experience complications, like respiratory distress syndrome, that necessitate admission to the neonatal intensive care unit.
Long-term health implications.
Children born to parents with PCOS have a higher risk of developing certain chronic health conditions later in life, such as obesity and diabetes.

editorialText.title: Optimizing fertility with PCOS

children.text: Not everyone with PCOS will experience these complications, and it’s worth repeating that many women with PCOS have successful, healthy pregnancies. Thankfully, there are several commonsense and effective strategies, including lifestyle modifications and medications, that can greatly increase the likelihood of ovulating and becoming pregnant if you suffer from PCOS.

Lifestyle modifications almost always begin with getting regular exercise and eating a healthy diet, generally full of protein-rich, low-carbohydrate foods; high-fiber fruits and vegetables; whole grains; and healthy fats and oils. Cutting out caffeine, nicotine, and alcohol are also common recommendations. Weight management through exercise and dietary changes can vastly improve insulin sensitivity and hormonal imbalances, stabilize blood sugar levels, and make fertility easier to achieve, often without additional intervention.

When lifestyle changes alone don’t lead to pregnancy, healthcare providers often recommend medication to improve fertility and promote ovulation, including metformin and/or clomiphene citrate (Clomid) alone or in combination. Metformin, an insulin-lowering drug and mainstay in type 2 diabetes management, has been shown to improve fertility and ovulation, and reverse endocrine abnormalities in women with PCOS. .Metformin is also a drug-of-choice for women with PCOS because it also has positive effects on other PCOS symptoms, including menstrual irregularities, anovulation (lack of ovulation), hirsutism, and obesity. And during pregnancy, metformin might also be protective against insulin resistance, gestational diabetes, and a first trimester pregnancy loss. Clomiphene citrate (Clomid) is an important ovulation induction agent that works by stimulating hormones necessary for follicle development. Letrozole, another medication that induces ovulation, is sometimes used when clomiphene citrate is ineffective.

editorialText.title: Seeking preconception care

children.text: As always, it’s important for people with PCOS to consult with an experienced and trustworthy healthcare provider before trying to conceive. Your provider can help assess your fertility status, manage PCOS symptoms, and address underlying health conditions to improve your chances of getting pregnant, staying pregnant, and having a healthy pregnancy. And since PCOS is associated with other long-term health risks, like type 2 diabetes, endometrial cancer, and cardiovascular disease, it’s even more critical for these people to share their fertility journeys and health histories with their provider before conception so they can make the best and most informed decisions about their health and care.

editorialText.title: Pregnancy care for people with PCOS

children.text: Close collaboration with a healthcare provider, strict adherence to prenatal care, lifestyle management, and taking a prenatal vitamin that offers folic acid are key components of ensuring a healthy pregnancy for women with PCOS. Since women with PCOS who become pregnant are more vulnerable to certain pregnancy-related complications, like gestational diabetes and preeclampsia, regular prenatal office visits and routine screenings are essential for recognizing the earliest signs of these conditions. Your care team will closely monitor your blood pressure, blood glucose levels, and weight gain, measure the progression of your pregnancy, and address any issues that might contribute to miscarriage or preterm labor.

editorialText.title: Next steps for PCOS treatment

children.text: PCOS is a complex and prevalent condition that requires a comprehensive and personalized approach. The impact of PCOS extends beyond the bounds of reproductive health, it impacts metabolic and cardiovascular health too. Some symptoms, like infertility, obesity, and unwanted hair growth, can lead to social stigma and even cause anxiety and depression. Consultation with a healthcare provider or fertility specialist is essential for a thorough evaluation, diagnosis, and a personalized treatment plan aimed at restoring hormonal balance, managing symptoms, and regulating ovulation.
So, whether you’re already pregnant, trying, undecided, or not planning to have kids, your journey matters—and so does your story.

metaInformation.metaTitle: PCOS and Pregnancy: Embracing the Unique Path to Motherhood

children.text: If you’re like most people, you’ve probably heard of
polycystic ovary syndrome

(PCOS) but don’t know exactly what it is. If so, you’re not alone. Even healthcare professionals don’t fully understand this common endocrine disorder that affects up to 10% of women in their reproductive years.
The array of symptoms and list of causes associated with PCOS make this condition challenging to diagnose and treat. It’s a confusing disorder, and there is a lot we still don’t know.
But the medical community does know some important things about PCOS that can be useful if you’ve been diagnosed with the condition and are looking for ways to manage it effectively.

It knows that PCOS is characterized by a constellation of symptoms, including irregular menstrual cycles, elevated androgens (male hormones), infertility, excessive ‘male pattern’ hair growth, weight gain, acne, and multiple small cysts on the ovaries.
It knows that PCOS is likely caused by a combination of genetic, lifestyle, and environmental factors.

And it knows this: Diet and nutrition play significant roles in managing PCOS, reducing symptoms, and improving metabolic issues – like insulin resistance and inflammation – that are often associated with the disorder.

Keep reading to better understand the relationship between PCOS and diet and nutrition and to find out how you can use food to fight back against this all-too-common condition and reclaim your health once and for all.

editorialText.title: What Diet is Best for Managing PCOS?

children.text: When it comes to PCOS, there is no ‘one size, fits all’ approach to diet and nutrition. Everybody and every body are unique, and the best strategy involves finding a diet that works for you and staying flexible in your efforts to achieve the main goals of managing PCOS through diet and nutrition: losing weight, reducing inflammation, and improving insulin resistance.

Studies have shown that losing weight can dramatically improve metabolic and reproductive health for people with PCOS regardless of which diet they choose. Even modest weight loss (5-10% of total body weight) can help balance hormones, regulate menstrual cycles, restore ovulation, and increase pregnancy rates.

Chronic, low-grade inflammation is another factor that is often seen in people with PCOS, and research shows that incorporating more anti-inflammatory foods into the diet can also reduce symptoms of PCOS and improve overall health.

Experts generally agree that a healthy PCOS diet should include whole foods with minimal processing, healthy fats, lean proteins, low-fat milk products, and non-starchy, low-glycemic carbohydrates. They also recommend eating several small, balanced meals throughout the day instead of three bigger meals to prevent food cravings, snacking, and over-eating and to keep insulin levels in a steady state in between meals.
Here are the key components of a PCOS diet:
Whole grain bread, pasta, and cereals
Brown rice, overnight oats, and quinoa are rich in fiber and antioxidants, which can help reduce inflammation.
High-fiber fruits and vegetables

Colorful fruits and vegetables are high in antioxidants and phytochemicals that have anti-inflammatory properties. Apples, plums, berries, leafy greens, tomatoes, broccoli, and cauliflower are all great choices.
Lean proteins

Chicken and turkey (without skin), eggs, and plant-based sources such as nuts, beans, legumes, nut butters, avocado, and hummus are all approved picks. The omega-3 fatty acids found in fatty fishes, like salmon and tuna, have anti-inflammatory effects and should be included in your diet too.
Low-fat dairy
Low-fat, low-lactose dairy products like cottage cheese or Greek yogurt and non-dairy alternatives such as coconut, almond, and rice milk should be on the menu.
Spices and herbs
Certain spice and herbs, such as turmeric, ginger, cinnamon, and garlic have potent anti-inflammatory properties and can easily be added into meals.
Healthy fats
Think olive oil, flaxseed oil, coconut oil and seeds – flax, chia, and sunflower.
Beverages
Caffeinated drinks and alcohol can exacerbate PCOS symptoms and rack up calories quickly. Water is your best bet. Tea and seltzer with a splash of juice are other smart options.

editorialText.title: The Impact of Diet on Insulin Resistance in Women with PCOS

children.text: Losing weight

and following a PCOS diet can help with another key component of this disorder: insulin resistance. Insulin is a hormone that regulates blood sugar levels and insulin resistance is a condition in which the body’s cells don’t respond effectively to it.
As a result, the pancreas pumps out excess insulin, which stimulates the ovaries to produce more androgens (male hormones) and cause many PCOS symptoms, like irregular periods and hirsutism.

Insulin resistance also contributes to weight gain and leads to a vicious cycle. Excess weight causes insulin resistance. Insulin resistance causes weight gain. So, besides losing weight and reducing inflammation, addressing insulin resistance through diet and nutrition is also critical in managing PCOS.

editorialText.title: Dietary Strategies for Managing Insulin Resistance

children.text: There are four main strategies to keep in mind when trying to follow a PCOS diet that helps manage insulin resistance:
Low Glycemic Index (GI) Foods

The glycemic index is a measure of how quickly a food raises blood sugar levels. Foods with a low GI are digested and absorbed more slowly, which leads to a gradual rise in blood sugar and insulin and helps improve insulin sensitivity.
High-fiber
Fiber slows down the digestion and absorption of carbohydrates, leading to a gradual increase in blood sugar and insulin levels.
Balanced macronutrient intake

Balancing carbohydrates with lean proteins and healthy fats can help stabilize blood sugar levels. Protein slows down the digestion of carbohydrates and healthy fats can also help improve insulin sensitivity.
It's important to include a source of protein and healthy fat in each meal and snack to keep blood sugar levels stable.
Avoiding refined carbohydrates
Refined carbohydrates, such as white bread, pastries, and sugary snacks, can cause rapid spikes in blood sugar and insulin levels, worsening insulin resistance.

editorialText.title: Foods to Avoid if You Have PCOS

children.text: People with PCOS should avoid foods that can spike blood sugar or cause inflammation. The following foods should be avoided if you have PCOS:
Baked goods – bread, cereal, pastries – made with refined white flour
Starchy vegetables (potatoes, corn)
White rice
Red meat and processed meats (lunch meat, hot dogs, sausage, bacon)
Full-fat dairy products
Fried foods
Dried fruit
Frozen meals and snacks
Unhealthy fats, like butter and margarine
Commercial salad dressing, marinades, seasonings
Sugary drinks – soda, fruit juices, energy drinks

editorialText.title: Supplements for PCOS: Do They Help?

children.text: Besides dietary changes, nutritional supplements may help ease some PCOS symptoms. Research is limited, so be sure to consult your healthcare provider before starting a new supplement regimen.
Inositol has been shown to improve insulin sensitivity, reduce androgen levels, and promote regular ovulation.
Omega-3 fatty acids, found in fish oil and flaxseed oil, have anti-inflammatory properties and may help reduce insulin resistance and lower androgen levels.
Vitamin D deficiency is common in women with PCOS and has been linked to insulin resistance and other metabolic issues.
Chromium is a trace mineral that plays a role in carbohydrate and fat metabolism. Some studies suggest chromium supplementation may improve insulin sensitivity and reduce carb cravings.
Conclusion

Maintaining a healthy weight is a critical aspect of managing PCOS, and even modest weight loss can significantly improve insulin sensitivity, reduce inflammation, and restore ovulatory function and healthy androgen levels. There are many strategies used to treat PCOS, but diet and nutrition provide one of the most practical and profound ways to ward off the unwanted and unhealthy symptoms of this condition. Following a PCOS-approved diet and practicing mindful eating are important steps to take, but getting regular exercise (aerobic, strength training), improving sleep quality, and reducing stress are also part of the bigger picture of improving PCOS and improving your overall health and wellness in the process.

children.text: Symptoms we can help with
Cramps Or Pelvic Pain

Cramps or pelvic pain can be uncomfortable, disruptive, and hard to ignore. A visit for these symptoms helps uncover what’s going on, whether it’s cycle-related changes, muscle tension, or an underlying condition, and creates a personalized plan to relieve pain and help you feel more in control of your body.
Abnormal Bleeding

From PMS to PCOS, heavy periods to no periods, we can help you evaluate and manage your cycle. Heavy bleeding, clots, and spotting can be symptoms of an underlying issue, and they are never something you have to just "deal with." We'll work together to diagnose what's causing the abnormal bleeding and find a treatment for you. Stop worrying about your cycle and start working toward a healthier, more predictable flow.
Vaginal Infection

From chronic yeast infections to Bacterial Vaginosis (BV) to that "not-sure-what-but-something-is-up" feeling, we've got you covered with diagnosis, treatment, and prevention. We know these symptoms are frustrating, often recurrent, and rarely discussed in a supportive setting. At Tia, we provide expert care to find the root cause, address imbalances, and create a personalized plan to get you back to feeling comfortable and confident, without judgment.

children.text: Integrated women’s health, all in one place
The Problem

Healthcare should be a relationship, not a series of disconnected transactions. But all too often, it's not. Gynecological and sexual health is too often siloed, forcing you to go to separate providers for your annual exam, a painful sex consult, or a new birth control prescription. Many women feel isolated and alone, passed from one specialist to the next without a central provider who truly knows their entire health story. We know these frustrations are real. In a 2022 report, nearly
one in three women said they felt dismissed by their doctor.

Our providers are trained to connect the dots between symptoms like irregular periods or painful sex and your overall physical, mental, and emotional health. Why Gynecological and Sexual Health matters for women
Your body is a connected system.

Gynecological symptoms aren't isolated; they’re often signals from the rest of your body. A dedicated care team helps connect the dots, understanding how stress, sleep, and overall health can influence things like your period or pelvic pain, sexual wellness, or irregular bleeding.
Your symptoms deserve to be taken seriously.

You're not imagining it. For too long, women's pain—from endometriosis to chronic pelvic issues—has been dismissed and minimized. You deserve care from providers trained to understand the full picture of women's reproductive and sexual health who will listen actively and never brush off your concerns
Prevention is the foundation of lifelong health.

Building a relationship with a trusted primary care team means catching problems early, from getting your cervical cancer screening (Pap test) on time to addressing changes in your reproductive health , ensuring you stay healthier for the long term.

testimonials.Content: My experience at Tia was exceptional from start to finish. As someone with a 9–5 job, being able to book a 6 PM appointment for an OBGYN concern was a huge relief. From check-in, Angie made me feel welcomed and cared for, and Stephanie was professional, gentle with the blood draw, and made sure I felt comfortable the whole time. I saw Jamee, the nurse practitioner, who was incredibly supportive and knowledgeable. She took her time answering all my questions and helped me feel at ease with clear explanations and genuine care. Tia delivers warm, respectful, patient-focused care, and I’ve already started recommending it to friends and colleagues. Here, you truly feel heard and supported.
testimonials.Title: Clarissa B., Tia Patient

testimonials.Content: I had my IUD removed and replaced today which is probably one of the most uncomfortable procedures EVER but the care team made sure that my comfort was prioritized. In addition to the luxurious robe and beautiful interiors I've come to love from Tia, I was provided with a heating pad, white noise machine, and lavender essential oil to smell during my procedure. My NP was extremely thorough and asked if I had any questions and walked me through every step as it was happening. I felt very taken care of! As soon as I got home I was messaged aftercare information! It’s clear that Tia thinks through every step of their care and prioritizes comfort along with exceptional healthcare.
testimonials.Title: Chloe M., Tia Patient

testimonials.Content: Amazing experience with Tia! The providers are compassionate and the office beautiful and efficient. The care is excellent with a focus on the whole person, offering mental health and hormone treatments as well as primary women's healthcare. Used acupuncture with my procedure and couldn't believe the difference it made. 10/10 recommend Tia to anyone
testimonials.Title: Alexis R.,Tia Patient

testimonials.Content: I’ve read many reviews about this facility, but my experience was truly exceptional. I’ve had a lot of trauma around women’s health and avoided gynecologic care for years, but Tia completely changed that. Dr. Ryan Darvish is the first provider who made me feel genuinely safe and cared for. After an abnormal pap, I went through several difficult procedures, and he guided me through each one with compassion, clear communication, and a focus on keeping me comfortable. The same care and support were present during my IUD removal and replacement, which he made nearly painless. I’ve never felt so at ease during appointments that once felt overwhelming. Dr. Darvish is truly a gift, and I recommend him to anyone with fear or trauma around gynecologic care. Tia is now the only place I’ll go for my health needs.
testimonials.Title: Emily F., Tia Patient

testimonials.Content: I cannot say enough good things about my experience at Tia Clinic in Studio City, thanks to Jamee and Alauna. Their customer service was truly unmatched—every interaction was thoughtful, kind, and professional. Jamee was extremely patient with me, taking the time to answer every single one of my many questions without hesitation. She made me feel heard and valued, which is so rare these days. Both Jamee and Alauna went above and beyond to make me feel comfortable throughout my visit.  I’ve officially found my gyno for life with Jamee, and I couldn’t be more grateful for the care and support I received. If you’re looking for a team that genuinely cares about their patients, look no further than Tia Clinic.

children.text: Primary Care at Tia is women-focused, expanding beyond gynecological practices. We require a
whole-health practice

to broaden the collective definition of women’s health and well-being. Women’s health is a whole lot more than reproductive wellness or gynecology and it necessitates a whole lot more than an annual Pap test.
Whole Women's Health
Women’s health is more than gynecology.

At present, our healthcare system is organized by specialists whose expertise lie not in comprehensive or preventative healthcare, but in the singular management or intervention of disease. This creates a fragmented health system for patients, and particular for women.
In an effort to address this schism Tia is organized to address all of your health needs
under one roof

with your primary care and gynecology fully integrated. With this system we are able to diagnose, treat and prevent disease in women’s health. We do this through cultivating comprehensive women’s health providers who are trained to connect all of the dots that comprise female health and its nuances, complexity and interconnectedness.
Did you know?
Your Primary Care at Tia looks at the whole you, considering your unique milleui of female hormones.
Health as a whole should be the driving force behind women’s healthcare at large. Women’s healthcare cannot operate as a one-size-fits-all, it must be personalized.

children.text: Symptoms we can help with
Cold or Flu Like Symptoms

Feeling run down, congested, or achy and not sure if it’s just a cold or something more? A visit for cold and flu symptoms helps identify what’s going on, ease discomfort, and create a personalized plan so you can recover faster and get back to feeling like yourself.
Ear Issues

Experiencing ear pain, pressure, ringing, or changes in hearing? A visit for ear issues helps uncover what’s causing your symptoms and provides a personalized plan to relieve discomfort, restore balance, and help you feel like yourself again.
Sleep Disturbances

Having trouble falling asleep, staying asleep, or waking up feeling unrested? A visit for sleep disturbances helps uncover what’s disrupting your rest and creates a personalized plan to improve sleep quality, restore energy, and help you feel like yourself again.
Stomach Pain & Issues

Dealing with stomach pain, bloating, nausea, or ongoing discomfort? A visit for stomach pain issues helps identify what’s causing your symptoms and builds a personalized plan to ease pain, support digestion, and help you feel like yourself again.

modules.title: Where you can get dedicated care

children.text: Integrated women’s health, all in one place
The Problem

Healthcare should be a relationship, not a series of disconnected transactions. But all too often, it's not. Many women feel isolated and alone, passed from one specialist to the next without a central provider who truly knows their entire health story.
We know these frustrations are real. In a 2022 report, nearly one in three women said they felt dismissed by their doctor and that number climbs to 46% among women ages 18-35,
reporting a negative interaction with a healthcare provider
. This can lead to missed connections between symptoms, overlooked diagnoses, and the frustrating feeling that no one is truly listening.

items.signupSubcopy: We need some basic info to find you the right primary care doctor and book your first visit.

children.text: Takes 3–5 minutes to create an account • No membership fee to book • Insurance accepted
Tia’s Solution

We believe women deserve care that treats the whole person. Our primary care gives women one connected place for their whole health,from a cold to chronic conditions. Our providers take time to understand your physical, mental, and emotional health to create a personalized plan built around you.
Our approach helps women live healthier, more vibrant lives:
70% of Tia’s members hadn’t had a preventative exam in the year before joining Tia. After joining,
they complete life-saving screenings at rates 2-3x the national average.
88.2% of Tia patients were screened for depression, nearly
double the national benchmark
because we see mental health as a core part of your health.
It's healthcare that's proactive, not just reactive, designed to help you get well and stay well for the long term.

children.text: Getting started
A three-step process for getting started at Tia
Getting started is easy:
1. Check your insurance coverage
We’ll validate your insurance details when you sign up for Tia Essential or Tia and will confirm your in-network status before going forward.
2. Schedule your first appointment

Most patients begin with our new patient establishment of care visit to set the foundation for their health journey. During this time, we'll explore your current health concerns, goals, preferences, and health history.
3. Get started on your care journey

For those in need of an annual preventative exam, your provider may recommend this as a first step. If you have different needs, we'll collaborate with you to develop a care plan that works for you and your goals.

children.text: Allison is a board-certified Nurse Practitioner specializing in sexual and reproductive health. Passionate about reproductive justice and cultivating nonjudgemental medical spaces for all bodies, Allison uses active listening, empathy, sex-positivity, and humor to establish and maintain relationships with Tia members. They have extensive experience working in marginalized communities (e.g., those living with mental illness/ substance use disorders, abuse survivors, LGBTQ+ individuals, immigrants and their first generation family members) and are particularly excited to use Tia's innovative virtual platform to increase access in these spaces.

children.text: Women’s Health Clinic in New York City

Why go to multiple doctors’ offices—primary care clinic here, gynecologist there—when you can have everything you need at one women’s health clinic in New York City? At Tia, we offer comprehensive, holistic support from compassionate primary care providers, gynecologists, mental health specialists, and wellness practitioners who are here for your whole health.

With locations in the Manhattan (Soho) and Brooklyn (Williamsburg), we’re making it easier for women across New York City to access personalized care that prioritizes your health. Whether you prefer in-person visits or virtual appointments, our team works together to support your mental and physical health—across your whole life.

children.text: Today, we reopen the physical doors to the Tia Clinic. We will be providing essential services to women that must be delivered in-person and can no longer be delayed, such as Pap tests, pelvic exams, IUD insertions, and other time-sensitive procedures. We will continue to provide all other services virtually, as a commitment to delivering the highest quality care to women across the continuum (in-clinic, virtual, and all of the moments in-between).

editorialText.title: The decision to close and reopen

children.text: The decision to close our doors was deliberate, so too is the choice to reopen. Tia provides essential services, meaning, we never had to close, but as NYC and the world came to a pause, we felt our operations at the clinic needed to pause too. With information changing in real-time, we took an evidence-based approach in our decision-making and elected to temporarily close our physical doors. We were able to effectively divert our care to virtual offerings which enabled us to continue to provide care to our patients. The decision to close was made for the safety of our patients, our staff, and the NYC healthcare system (at the time, inundated by volume and short on supplies). Closing our clinic also meant minimizing the spread of Covid-19 and reserving personal protective equipment for the frontlines.

77 days later, we are grateful to see that the circumstances of the Covid-crisis have changed. And now, because of the heroic efforts of the frontline medical community, and the Tia community who heeded the call to adjust their behaviors to curb the crisis, we are re-opening to provide essential care that, quite simply, cannot be done virtually.

All Tia providers were voluntarily signed-up to work on the frontline and were prepared to mobilize to acute care in NYC as needed. With Virtual Care we could uphold our commitment to our patients who needed care while practicing strict social distancing.

imageCaption.caption: We can no longer delay the essential services we provide to females.

editorialText.title: Here’s what we’re doing to ensure safety for both patients and providers at the Tia Clinic

children.text: Safety begins before your appointment
To prevent exposure for our patients and staff, all Covid-related concerns will be addressed virtually (read: not at the Tia Clinic)
All patients coming into the clinic will answer a pre-visit health screening via text to assess symptoms before arrival
Appointment check-ins will be done 100% virtually
Safety first at the Tia Clinic
Strict social distancing throughout
One person at a time in the elevator
Spaced out seating in our living room
Staggered appointments (a limited number of patients will be allowed in the clinic at a time)
Extra safety protocols
Upon arrival, every patient will receive a mask, a temperature check, and asked to wash their hands
We are keeping the Tia Clinic extra clean, by disinfecting all counter surfaces and medical equipment after each patient, throughout the day, and every evening with EPA-approved products
All Tia staff members screened daily, testing regularly for Covid-19 and providing personal protective gear for respiratory and contact precautions

editorialText.title: What reopening means for Tia

children.text: Over the past ten weeks, temporarily operating in a virtual-only state, it’s been amazing to see our patients and providers rapidly adopt new technology and fundamentally shift their mental model. We’ve seen an enlightened recognition that virtual care does not have to be a “lesser-than” version of in-person care, or limited to “quick fixes” and prescription refills. Rather, when designed with intention and relationship-based (as opposed to transact-based), virtual care has the potential to facilitate deep dialogue and human connection — in many regards, more so than an in-person visit, during which an honest conversation to evaluate your health with your provider can be eclipsed by exam tables, needles, and stirrups.

The Tia Clinic’s reopening signifies our investment in the ever-expanding care continuum — a virtually-integrated care model that is both online and offline. A model for the future of healthcare that’s all about delivering the right service in the right place at the right time and using Tia’s technology to connect the moments in-between.
We hope to see you soon for a virtual or in-clinic appointment with Tia.

children.text: Throughout Tia’s trajectory from digital to physical, and from a pure tech company to a tech-enabled clinical services business, there’s been one unifying thread that’s guided our approach throughout: relationships. We believed then and we believe now that women want
someone
, not
something

to manage their health. That for women, quality healthcare means a real face and a safe space that makes you feel seen, heard and compassionately cared for — online and offline. And that Tia’s mission and foremost innovation is building a relationship-based care model at a time when “relationships” in healthcare are evermore rare.
Eight weeks since opening our first brick-and-mortar Tia Clinic, we’ve proven that women really,
really

want a differentiated relationship with the healthcare system, and that they are excited about Tia’s potential to deliver it to them. Despite the influx in demand and pressure to rapidly grow to service more members, we recently made the call to deliberately slow down before scaling up. We continue to believe that changing women’s healthcare is a marathon, and not a sprint, and that unchecked “hockey stick growth” cannot be applied to clinical care delivery when real human lives are at play. Instead, we are taking the approach that growth must be deliberate, thoughtful, and obsessively monitored to ensure care quality is never — not even for a second — sacrificed because of startup “growing pains.”

editorialText.title: Clarifying the role of technology

children.text: In this explosive era of direct to consumer healthcare companies, we are repeatedly asked —
what is Tia?
— a technology company, a direct to consumer brand, or a healthcare services business? To this, we say we are not one, but all three — or more accurately, a relationship company above all else.

More tactically, our approach rests on amplifying best-in-class clinical care with innovations in technology and experience design to strengthen — not replace — the canonical patient <> provider relationship. More than an Instagrammable waiting room, or “tech for tech’s sake”, we are laser-focused on interweaving technology and design into the patient and provider experience — not as a “wrapper around,” but in service of clinical care.
In the words of our users, there’s an intangible, emotional quality that results from the interwoven-ness of technology, brand and
amazing
clinicians all-in-one:
Tia patient reviews
In short, Tia’s model is about a sum greater than its individual parts. Remove the tech, remove the brand, or remove the care, and the whole thing disintegrates.

editorialText.title: The necessity for innovation in women’s health

children.text: While we believe that every woman
should

have a deep, loved and trusted 1:1 relationship with her provider, delivering this at scale and at an affordable price-point within the confines of the existing healthcare system is rife with challenges. Specifically, the dominant fee-for-service reimbursement models, women’s health provider shortages, and rapidly changing consumer demands make the historic 1:1 relationship with a provider a utopian concept of the past — increasingly limited to very expensive concierge medicine practices inaccessible to the mass market.
While we know Tia is not
yet
a universally-accessible solution that works for every woman with
any

health need across the socioeconomic spectrum, we are committed to building a best-in-class relationship-based care model where women don’t have to “choose” between the trifecta of care quality, convenience and insurance coverage. Instead at the Tia Clinic, women can get evidence-based integrative care in a non “speed dating” environment — for the price of what you pay for Netflix or Spotify each month. To deliver on this promise involves shifting the historic bi-directional patient <> provider relationship to a triangular one, with Tia as the bridge or liaison between a patient and her provider(s):

editorialText.title: Productizing this new care model

children.text: While Tia started out as the go-to “wing-woman” for women seeking health information before and after the doctor’s office, the move into real-world care delivery requires that Tia become the “wing-woman” for providers, too. In the words of Tia’s Chief Medical Officer, Dr. Stephanie McClellan:

“Tia must become both healthcare translator, care navigator and research-obsessed thinker. For patients, Tia should act as ‘your best friend in medical school’ who explains things to you without dumbing things down. And for providers, Tia needs to be their ‘full-time research assistant,’ providing data-driven, evidence-based recommendations that at times, may be non-obvious, but clinically important. To create a meaningful patient <> provider relationship in the context of the pressures intrinsic to the modern medical system, Tia can’t just play one of these roles, Tia needs to excel at all of them.”
From a product perspective, this means building tools that:
#1: Give the patient more control and agency over her healthcare:
Tia patients control their own health record & can share their cycle, health & wellness data with their Care Team to personalize their experience.
#2: Equip providers to spend more time interfacing with patients (and less time interfacing with the EHR)

TiaMD (Tia’s “doctor facing” iPad app) flags relevant health data to providers ahead of a patient visit. During Tia appointments, this information is visualized on a “third screen” for the patient & provider to view in tandem.
#3: Facilitate transparency and collaborative care
During Tia appointments, patients & providers build a collaborative care plan personalized to the patient’s goal.

editorialText.title: Why people + tech > tech alone

children.text: Patient reviews like these reinforce our belief that the reimagined patient <> provider relationship of the future is at the nexus of technology, design and clinical care.

But, we’ve also learned that we need to make further investments in the underlying service model to support this new paradigm — particularly outside of Tia exam room walls. We know that technology alone can’t cut it as an effective bridge between the patient and provider. Instead, successful solutions involve both people
and
technology paired together.

As a patient-first company who believes that transparency is the foundation of trust, here are the chief points of feedback we’ve heard from our members the past eight weeks, and the ways we are working to address these wants and needs before releasing new memberships:
#1: “I want more appointment times that work with my schedule!”

While non-traditional doctor’s office hours optimized for the working woman have always been part of our care promise, we opened with limited availability for early morning, evening and weekend gynecology appointments, which booked up far more quickly than we anticipated.

To address the demand for these hours, and the higher-than-expected utilization we’ve seen amongst our patient population, we have hired two additional women’s health nurse practitioners to our Care Team to support an expanded schedule. Starting this week, the Tia Clinic has:
“Pre-Work” morning appointments starting at 7:40AM Monday — Friday
“Post-Work” evening appointments ‘till 8PM Mondays, Tuesdays & Thursdays
Saturday gynecology appointments 9AM — 3PM once a month starting May 11th (and acupuncture every Saturday!)
Designated same and next day appointments for time-sensitive issues (such as UTIs or strep throat) that are released 24 hours ahead of time

As we further expand our Care Team with an additional MD hire over the coming weeks, we will continue to expand our appointment availability, upholding our commitment to convenience and accessibility without compromising care quality or overloading our providers.
#2: “I love the experience I get at the Tia Clinic, but want that personalized care to extend before and after my visit.”
To date, we’ve struggled to ensure patients get the timely communication they desire,
while also

allowing our providers to stay focused on patient care in our exam rooms. To address this, we’re making significant changes to both our staffing model and supporting tech products to improve patient communications pre and post-Tia appointments:
Specifically, we are introducing:
Care Coordinators

a dedicated human face assigned to members when they join to help them navigate every touch point of Tia Care. From questions about scheduling and billing, to referrals and labs, this person will act as our members’ “personal concierge” to streamline all communications between a patient and her provider team at Tia. We believe the addition of Care Coordinators will facilitate higher touch communications, faster response times, and enable continuity of care — without overburdening our providers.
Chat with the Care Team

— and in a few weeks, we are launching a private, secure inbox for ALL communications with the Tia Clinic. These past few weeks, we’ve seen that administrative questions (“I need to reschedule my appointment”) can quickly become clinical (“Do I need an ultrasound?”), and clinical questions can quickly become administrative. We are streamlining communications with a single inbox, staffed by each member’s personal Care Coordinator.
A single front door to the digital Tia Care experience

— from text messages to the app to the Tia Clinic Home on the web, we know it can be confusing to know where to go for information from Tia. To solve this, we are introducing a single doorway to all-things Tia Clinic where members can book appointments, edit their health record, find pre-appointment reminders (and soon, chat with the Care Team, too!) — accessible from
our iPhone app
or our website. We will continue to use text message as a notification and reminder system to ensure members don’t miss a beat.
#3: “There are so many different services at Tia from workshops to events to different types of gynecology and wellness appointments. But, what’s right for me?”

Today, orientation is commonplace in college, the workplace, conferences, you name it. We would never think about bringing on a new employee without “onboarding” he or she to our company’s values, processes, and workflows. Yet, in healthcare — one of the most complicated, but important systems we interact with — we leave patients to figure things out on their own, and all-too-often, not until a crisis strikes.

To address this, we are introducing a program, free for all Tia members, called “Well-Woman Orientation” — quite literally, an orientation to the Tia Clinic, our care philosophy, Care Team, and service offering.

Starting Wednesday, May 8th, Tia members can sign up for a 90-minute Well-Woman Orientation, during which they will meet our Care Team and other members, set individualized health goals, and design a plan to achieve them with the support of Tia and our community.

While the past eight weeks have reinforced that women want, need and deserve better care, we are dually humbled and evermore motivated by the challenge inherent to delivering that experience — first and foremost to our current members, and slowly and sustainably, to future members in NYC and around the country. As founders, we are excited by the sheer
excitement
we’ve seen women express about engaging in their health in a new way. Most importantly though, we are keenly aware of the responsibility that comes along with that.
To us, the answer to translating an ambitious vision into action lies in the most sacred part of any relationship:
listening

. To our members current and future — we pledge to always listen faster than we move, and evolve our interwoven web of technology, design and clinical operations with the utmost intention. This, we believe, is our ultimate mission.
Ready to become a Tia member?

children.text: What would your Care Team be like — look like and work like — if it was designed by women and for women?
Besides the obvious of being made up of all women, here are a few things we heard from our community of co-creators:
“Going to the doctor makes me feel like the child of divorced parents. My PCP doesn’t talk to my gyno who definitely doesn’t talk to my acupuncturist and I’m stuck in the middle!”
“My doctors make me feel so patronized… like I don’t have a brain or an ability to make an informed decision for my own health.”
“As a woman of color, I want to see people who look like me when I go to the doctor — especially at the gynecologist.”
When we set out to hire our own Care Team for the first NYC Tia Clinic, we took these pain points to heart, and built a modern medical home with:
#1: A single Care Team that works together for you — not against you.
#2: Multiple disciplines and modalities under one roof — and most important of all, one health record and one set of patient notes!
#3: Care providers who explain why and not just what — who exist to serve as consultants in, and not dictators of YOUR health!
#4: A diverse Care Team that reflects the diversity of our members.
#5: A community and environment that makes not only our patients happy, but our providers happy, too!
Interested in our services?

main.badge: Virtual & In-Person Care

children.text: Integrated women's healthcare clinic
Culver City women’s clinic: primary care physicians & gynecologists

At the Tia Culver City women’s clinic, we make it easy for women to get comprehensive, compassionate care for their bodies and minds in one accessible location. Our team of experts—including primary care physicians, gynecologists, and acupuncturists—collaborate to support your whole health, from annual exams and acute issues to complicated chronic conditions.

Whether you're seeking wellness guidance, reproductive health support, or mental health services, we make it easy to get the care you need. With flexible options for both in-person visits and virtual appointments, caring for your whole self has never been more convenient.
Your modern medical home, in Culver City
Open Now

modules.title: Our Culver City team: primary care physicians, gynecologists and nurse practitioners

children.text: Primary care, gynecology, mental health services offered in Culver City
Insurances accepted at Tia Culver City

modules.optionalCTA: Learn about how insurance works at Tia

children.text: About Tia Culver City

Centrally located in the heart of downtown, Tia Culver City is a one-stop-shop for women's healthcare. This includes integrated primary care, gynecology, mental health, and wellness care. From the moment you enter through our doors, you’ll experience compassion, kindness and a new standard of care for women.

cells: Please give yourself extra time to find parking and avoid a late arrival! There is paid parking in the Platform structure off of Landmark Street. Tia offers free validation for your first hour.
When to arrive to the clinic before your appointment

Please arrive to your appointment 15 min early, in case you get lost or have trouble with traffic or parking. If you are late to your appointment, we may not be able to care for you and you may be charged a fee.

children.text: Why choose Tia Culver City?
An integrative Care Team specializing in women's health
We employ the highest quality providers that are vetted & trained in whole-person health with a focus on root cause diagnosis & lifestyle-based interventions
Healthcare whenever & wherever you need it
Virtual and in-person appointments on your schedule, Rx refills on the go & easy referrals to top specialists.
Personalized care with thoughtful touches
Tia takes the guesswork & coldness out of healthcare with dedicated Care Coordinators to decode bills & get you in-network referrals to clinics that feel warm & comfortable.
Culver City loves Tia

testimonials.Content: The whole experience, start to finish was wonderful! The space is beautiful, my doctor was so attentive, checkin and checkout were so easy.
testimonials.Title: Elyse

testimonials.Content: Great space, particularly the waiting room with the nice water options. Everyone from the front desk to the nurse to the doctor were very kind. Great that I was able to get my pap, breast exam, blood labs and flu shot all at once!
testimonials.Title: Lily

testimonials.Content: Going to Tia was the best experience ever. The staff was attentive and the best part is that they were all women!! I've never like going to the OB/GYN but going to Tia made it more enjoyable and less scary
testimonials.Title: TJ

testimonials.Content: Great attention, from the administrator at the front desk who helped me book an appointment for another day and showed me to a peaceful spot where I could wait. To the provider who was attentive, funny, a good listener and made me feel sooooo at ease
testimonials.Title: Monica

testimonials.Content: "It was SO amazing! That was the first time I felt like a psychiatrist really listened and understood everything I was saying. I was so used to providers not taking me seriously and I really felt supported and understood! "
testimonials.Title: Vasi

testimonials.Content: The environment was beyond inviting. Every single staff member made me feel welcome and at ease. The nurse and doctor that assisted me were very respectful of my anxiety and my needs. I felt valued and very well taken care of. I will 100% recommend Tia to anyone looking for medical assistance.
testimonials.Title: Alexa

children.text: Tia was created to be a one-stop-shop for all of your physical, mental, and emotional health needs. We also recognize that sometimes, women need specialty care that falls outside of the scope of our Care Teams. That’s why we’ve teamed up with Cedars-Sinai to enable all Tia members in Los Angeles seamless access to premier specialists and hospitals when needed.

children.text: I am a dedicated Family Nurse Practitioner that makes every patient feel like family! With 13 years of Adult Critical Care. I am originally from Philadelphia and have worked all throughout the country. The one consistent area in medicine I found was lacking tenderness was Women’s Health and Gynecology. I want to be a safe space for women to learn and love their bodies and see how powerful and special they are! I am passionate about representation in healthcare and culturally based care. Educating my patients to live their best most fulfilled life is my goal!

children.text: With a robust background in emergency medicine and a dedication to addressing chronic disease, I pursued advanced education to become a Nurse Practitioner specializing in primary care, with a focus on functional lifestyle, regenerative, and longevity medicine. Through my practice, I aim to cultivate a supportive environment where patients can take charge of their health and realize a life filled with vitality and fulfillment.

children.text: Women’s Health Clinic in Los Angeles

Why go to multiple doctors’ offices—primary care clinic here, gynecologist there—when you can have everything you need at one women’s health clinic in Los Angeles? At Tia, we offer comprehensive, holistic support from compassionate primary care providers, gynecologists, mental health specialists, and wellness practitioners who are here for your whole health.
Find a women’s clinic in Los Angeles for women's healthcare services

With locations in the Valley (Studio City, Pasadena), Culver City, Playa Vista), Silver Lake, and West Hollywood, we’re making it easier for women across Los Angeles to access personalized care that prioritizes your health. Whether you prefer in-person visits or virtual appointments, our team works together to support your mental and physical health—across your whole life.
Yes, we take insurance
Tia may not look like a typical doctor’s office, but you can still use your insurance for high-quality care—most members do!
We accept most major PPOs
Copays and deductibles still apply, just like any other provider
Your membership fee gets you top-quality, fast, easy access to expert providers, avoiding long waits
Los Angeles loves Tia

“Before coming to Tia, I was in constant pain. With my Tia providers, I developed a holistic plan—including low impact yoga, medicine adjustments, gut education, and mental health that has relieved my chronic pain and inflammation!”
—Alekza, Tia Member
“Tia feels revolutionary because it is. To have medical care where you and your well-being are actually prioritized feels like a breath of fresh air. So thankful for Tia.”
—Nicole, Tia Member

“From the moment I stepped into the clinic until the moment I left, everyone was unbelievably kind and helpful. The check-in process was so smooth, the waiting room was a dream, and my medical assistant and Nurse Practitioners were beyond incredible.”
—Molly, Tia Member

“Joining Tia last year was the best decision I've made for my physical and mental well-being in a while. They've got you covered from GYN visits, to acupuncture (my personal fave), to therapy as well as community events. It's a network of professional practitioners that's enriched my life in countless ways.”
—Dominika, Tia Member
“I am obsessed with the Tia Clinic. At each appointment, I feel cared for, listened to, and empowered with knowledge.”
—Lauren, Tia Member

Tia was created to be a one-stop-shop for all of your physical, mental, and emotional health needs. We also recognize that sometimes, women need specialty care that falls outside of the scope of our Care Teams. That’s why we’ve teamed up with Cedars-Sinai to enable all Tia members in Los Angeles seamless access to premier specialists and hospitals when needed.
To learn more about why we've partnered with Cedars-Sinai,
read our press release.
Visit one of our Los Angeles locations today

children.text: Molly is an Integrative Psychiatric Mental Health Nurse Practitioner who has been taking care of patients since 1997. She's worked in a variety of settings including the acute care inpatient setting, ER, Urgent Care, Student Health, and Outpatient Mental Health. Her approach is holistic, compassionate, trauma-informed, and patient-centered. Molly partners with you on your journey to more optimal mental health also taking into account lifestyle factors such as stress, food choices, movement, sleep hygiene, and community. She is a Meditation & Mindfulness instructor with over 20 years of dedicated meditation experience and includes Mindfulness-Based CBT and somatic parts-work in her sessions with patients. Molly is an author working on her second book and in her free time loves to surf nice small longboard waves in a warm ocean.

meta.metaTitle: Molly Brown, PMHNP | Tia Virtual Clinic, Psychiatric Nurse Practitioner

My passion is centered on empowering women to seize control of their health through comprehensive education on their bodies and personalized wellness plans, aimed at fostering longevity and preventing disease. Through my practice, I aim to cultivate a supportive environment where patients can take charge of their health and realize a life filled with vitality and fulfillment.

At the Tia Studio City women’s clinic, we make it easy for women to get comprehensive, compassionate care for their bodies and minds in one accessible location. Our team of experts—including primary care physicians, gynecologists, and acupuncturists—collaborate to support your whole health, from annual exams and acute issues to complicated chronic conditions.

Whether you're seeking wellness guidance, reproductive health support, or mental health services, we make it easy to get the care you need. With flexible options for both in-person visits and virtual appointments, caring for your whole self has never been more convenient.

testimonials.Content: The whole experience, start to finish was wonderful! The space is beautiful, my doctor was so attentive, checkin and checkout were so easy.
testimonials.Title: Elyse

testimonials.Content: Great space, particularly the waiting room with the nice water options. Everyone from the front desk to the nurse to the doctor were very kind. Great that I was able to get my pap, breast exam, blood labs and flu shot all at once!
testimonials.Title: Lily

testimonials.Content: Going to Tia was the best experience ever. The staff was attentive and the best part is that they were all women!! I've never like going to the OB/GYN but going to Tia made it more enjoyable and less scary
testimonials.Title: TJ

testimonials.Content: Great attention, from the administrator at the front desk who helped me book an appointment for another day and showed me to a peaceful spot where I could wait. To the provider who was attentive, funny, a good listener and made me feel sooooo at ease
testimonials.Title: Monica

testimonials.Content: "It was SO amazing! That was the first time I felt like a psychiatrist really listened and understood everything I was saying. I was so used to providers not taking me seriously and I really felt supported and understood! "
testimonials.Title: Vasi

testimonials.Content: The environment was beyond inviting. Every single staff member made me feel welcome and at ease. The nurse and doctor that assisted me were very respectful of my anxiety and my needs. I felt valued and very well taken care of. I will 100% recommend Tia to anyone looking for medical assistance.
testimonials.Title: Alexa

children.text: Nope! We have not figured out how to do a Pap on zoom (and we don't foresee this happening anytime soon either), but we can treat your UTI, yeast infection, review your health history, track the source of your pelvic pain, and so much more virtually — and, as needed, you can visit our in-person clinic for the physical exams.

faqs.question: Are you doing gynecology exams over the internet?

children.text: They are very similar. Telehealth typically means a one-time video or phone visit.
Virtual care

at Tia is more than that — it’s a connected care experience. From messaging with your provider to managing prescriptions and follow-ups, we make it easy to get the care you need and want. Think of it as your care team, just a tap away.

faqs.question: Is virtual care the same as telehealth?

children.text: No, the quality of care doesn’t change. Every virtual service we offer has been intentionally selected because we know we can deliver it with the same level of clinical quality, personalization, and compassion as in person. You’re in good hands, wherever you are.

faqs.question: Is the quality of care different between virtual or in person services?

children.text: Get the care you want and need with Tia
Still have questions about Tia? Call us at
You’re unique. Your care should be too.
At Tia, we provide personalized care that spans
ten specialties
, working to prevent disease and uncover root cause issues. From everyday wellness to complex concerns, our holistic approach puts you at the center of it all.
Questions about services?
1 Care Plan. 10 specialties. Fully connected.

Gynecology and sexual care, primary care, perimenopause & menopause, hormonal health, fertility & post partum support, weight & metabolic health, longevity, mental health, acupuncture, and skin care together under one roof—and in one coordinated Care Plan. No more bouncing between providers who never communicate. With Tia, every part of your health is treated as part of the whole, so you get care that actually works together—just like your body.
Gynecology and Sexual Health

From annual exams to navigating perimenopause and finding the right birth control, our expert providers take a holistic approach to care. We go beyond symptoms to see the bigger picture, offering ongoing support for concerns that need more than a one-time solution.
Our gynecological services include:

items: Well-woman exams (including pap tests & breast exams)
Contraception & family planning
Fertility care, from assessment to conception support
Perimenopause & menopause management
Abnormal bleeding
Vaginal, vulvar, or urinary symptoms (including STI testing & treatment)
PCOS, endometriosis, fibroids, & more

children.text: Symptoms we can help with
Cramps Or Pelvic Pain

Feeling low energy, mood swings, or sudden changes that do not feel like you? This new assessment helps connect the dots so you get clarity, a personalized plan, and the support to feel like yourself again.
Abnormal Bleeding

From PMS to PCOS, heavy periods to no periods, we can help you evaluate and manage your cycle. Heavy bleeding, clots, and spotting can be symptoms of an underlying issue, and they are never something you have to just "deal with." We'll work together to diagnose what's causing the abnormal bleeding and find a treatment for you. Stop worrying about your cycle and start working toward a healthier, more predictable flow.
Vaginal Infection

From chronic yeast infections to Bacterial Vaginosis (BV) to that "not-sure-what-but-something-is-up" feeling, we've got you covered with diagnosis, treatment, and prevention. We know these symptoms are frustrating, often recurrent, and rarely discussed in a supportive setting. At Tia, we provide expert care to find the root cause, address imbalances, and create a personalized plan to get you back to feeling comfortable and confident, without judgment.
Primary Care

We believe in preventing issues before they start so you can live as many healthy, vibrant years as possible. Our providers specialize in women’s health and partner with you to understand your unique needs—from preventative care to chronic condition management.
Our primary care services include:

items: Annual preventative exams & health screenings
Chronic condition management (e.g., diabetes, hypertension)
Abdominal & digestive issues
Sleep disturbances
Gender-affirming hormone therapy
Pain & headaches
Acute care for common illnesses & more

children.text: Symptoms we can help with
Cold or Flu Like Symptoms

Feeling run down, congested, or achy and not sure if it’s just a cold or something more? A visit for cold and flu symptoms helps identify what’s going on, ease discomfort, and create a personalized plan so you can recover faster and get back to feeling like yourself.
Ear Issues

Experiencing ear pain, pressure, ringing, or changes in hearing? A visit for ear issues helps uncover what’s causing your symptoms and provides a personalized plan to relieve discomfort, restore balance, and help you feel like yourself again.
Sleep Disturbances

Having trouble falling asleep, staying asleep, or waking up feeling unrested? A visit for sleep disturbances helps uncover what’s disrupting your rest and creates a personalized plan to improve sleep quality, restore energy, and help you feel like yourself again.
Stomach Pain & Issues

Dealing with stomach pain, bloating, nausea, or ongoing discomfort? A visit for stomach pain issues helps identify what’s causing your symptoms and builds a personalized plan to ease pain, support digestion, and help you feel like yourself again.
Perimenopause & Menopause

Your hormones don’t just “wind down” during perimenopause and menopause, they shift in ways that can impact everything from mood and sleep to weight, libido, and how you feel in your body. Our clinicians take a whole person approach to these changes, looking at the interplay between hormones, lifestyle, and symptoms to understand what’s really going on. We then build a personalized Care Plan to help you manage symptoms, protect long term health, and navigate this transition feeling informed, supported, and in control.

items: Navigate hormonal shifts clinically and naturally
Conventional and integrative treatments like HRT, nutrition, supplements, lifestyle support and acupuncture
Feel more like yourself again

children.text: Hormonal Health

Your hormones are powerful messengers that influence everything from your mood and energy to digestion, skin, and sexual health. When they’re out of balance, the symptoms can feel confusing, disconnected, or hard to pinpoint. Our clinicians take a comprehensive look at your hormonal ecosystem, exploring patterns across your cycle, lifestyle, stress, sleep, and labs to uncover what’s really driving your symptoms. Then we create a personalized plan to rebalance hormones, ease discomfort, and help you feel more in tune with your body every day.

items: Get answers for irregular cycle, bloating & cramps, or PMS
Understand how your hormones impact your whole health
A collaborative care team that connects the dots
Care designed for women at every life stage

children.text: Fertility & Postpartum Support

We're here for you, from questions about your fertility to trying to conceive, to navigating postpartum recovery. We provide expert care for your body and mind, without the pressure to pursue procedures or timelines that aren't right for you.

items: Get clarity on ovulation, cycle timing, and fertility challenges
Support for conception, pregnancy, and life after birth
Guidance for physical and emotional changes in the postpartum period
Evidence based support for breastfeeding, pelvic recovery, and mood

children.text: Weight Management & Metabolic Health

Your weight, energy, and metabolic health are deeply interconnected, so treating them in isolation rarely works. Our clinicians look at the full picture of what is happening in your body, from hormones and insulin sensitivity to nutrition, stress, sleep, and lifestyle patterns. By understanding how these factors shape your metabolism, we create a personalized plan that supports sustainable weight management, improved energy, and long term wellness tailored to your whole self.

items: For weight changes, insulin resistance, and metabolic health concerns
Personalized to your body and goals
Tools like GLP-1s, nutrition, and lifestyle when appropriate
Non-judgemental, personalized approach

children.text: Longevity

Longevity isn’t just about living longer, it’s about feeling better as you age. Your daily habits, hormones, metabolic health, and stress levels all influence how your body performs over time. Our clinicians look at the full picture, from labs and lifestyle to sleep, nutrition, and movement patterns, to understand what supports or accelerates aging in your unique system. Then we develop a personalized plan that helps you build strength, protect cognitive and metabolic health, and stay vibrant and resilient for the long haul.

items: Identify early markers that impact long term health and vitality
Personalized strategies for sleep, stress, nutrition, and movement
Care for your heart, brain, and beyond

children.text: Mental Health

Your mind and body are inextricably linked—so why should they be treated separately? Our expert clinicians take a comprehensive look at what’s going on with both—including physiological symptoms, aka how mental health feels in your body—to create a holistic Care Plan tailored to your whole self.
Our mental health offerings include:

items: Mental Health Assessments
Mental Health Medication Management
Psychiatry
Support Groups
Referrals to trusted partners for individual, couples, or family therapy

children.text: Offered at:
Tia Virtual Clinic
Acupuncture

Acupuncture, a science-backed Traditional Asian Medicine practice, offers a natural, holistic approach to addressing a wide range of concerns—from anxiety to fertility. Tia acupuncturists are women’s health experts who complement your care with treatments proven to work.
We offer acupuncture for these concerns
(and more!)

items: General health & wellbeing
Pain, headaches, & migraines
Menstrual cycle support
Mental health & stress
Abdominal & digestive issues
Fatigue
Perimenopause & menopause

children.text: Skin Care
Whether you're addressing a specific concern or just want to feel more confident in your skin, you’ll get trusted care from licensed Tia providers using science-backed, proven solutions.
We offer a range of custom medical and aesthetic skin care services, including:

items: Acne, rosacea, & eczema
Rashes or other skin changes
Custom, science-backed skincare regimens
Neurotoxins
Medical-grade chemical peels & microneedling

children.text: Symptoms we can help with
Acne, Rash or Skin Changes

Struggling with acne, rashes, or other skin changes that affect your comfort or confidence? A visit for skin issues helps uncover what’s driving your symptoms and creates a personalized plan to calm irritation, support healing, and help you feel like yourself again.
Ready to get the care you deserve—exceptional, compassionate, and all in one place?

Your health isn’t one thing, it’s everything. Whether you’re managing symptoms, navigating a life transition, or simply trying to understand your body better, Tia connects the dots so you don’t have to. Explore our services, meet your care team, and take the next step toward feeling informed, supported, and in control of your whole health. Ready when you are.

children.text: Annual Physical Exam

items: We focus on your preventative care for the year ahead.
We complete your comprehensive annual physical & well-woman exam.
We create your personalized preventative health plan, together.

children.text: Takes 3–5 minutes to create an account • No membership fee to book • Insurance accepted
Already a Tia member? Book here
What is this appointment?
First, we focus on your preventative health

This dedicated visit is the cornerstone of preventative care for women. We’ll review essential screenings, check in on your key health markers, and discuss your wellness goals—from nutrition to sleep—and build a proactive plan that helps you stay healthy.
Then, we complete your comprehensive exam

We combine your annual physical and well-woman exam into one convenient visit. This includes essential screenings like a Pap test (if you're due), breast exam, blood pressure check, and preventative blood work tailored to you.
Finally, we create your preventative plan together

More than just checking boxes, this visit should begin a conversation about what wellness means for you. We take your context and our knowledge to make a preventative care plan tailored to your unique needs and goals.
Who should have an Annual Physical Exam?
Everyone should have an Annual Physical Exam every year,

even if you are not due for a Pap test or don’t want a breast exam. These exams are focused on your holistic health and can be especially important if you’re managing a chronic condition, have notable family health history, or are moving through a significant life transition like planning for pregnancy, postpartum, or menopause.
What’s included in an Annual Physical Exam
A Preventative Health Discussion:
A focused conversation about your preventative health picture, including your lifestyle, goals, and any questions you have about staying healthy.
Physical Exam:
We’ll cover the essentials, including checking your vitals like blood pressure and a standard physical assessment.
Well-Woman Exam:
This includes a clinical breast exam and if you’re due, we'll perform a Pap test and HPV screening.
HPV Self-Collection Option:
For eligible patients who are unable to or uncomfortable with a traditional pelvic exam, we offer a self-collection option for HPV screening that you can do yourself, without a speculum.
Lab Work:
Your provider will order routine, preventative blood work based on your personal health risks and goals.
Personalized Care Plan:
We’ll wrap up by creating a preventative Care Plan that’s tailored to you, connecting this visit to your broader health journey.
Identify issues before they fester:
We work with you during this visit to identify health concerns and connect you to the right care to continue to work on those concerns going forward.
How an Annual Physical Exam can help

This check up is your dedicated time to feel empowered and in control of your health journey. It helps you stay on top of crucial preventative health screenings and get a clearer picture of your health with routine blood work. By partnering with your provider, you can better understand and manage your personal health risks, creating a proactive plan for prevention and long-term wellness
Insurance and Pricing
Is this covered by insurance?

Yes, most insurance plans cover one preventative exam per year at 100%. If it has been at least 12 months since your last annual and you stick to preventative topics, your insurance should cover the visit.
What if I discuss other health issues?

Your Annual Physical Exam is designed for preventative care. If you bring up new or specific health concerns (like a rash or headaches), your insurance provider may bill that portion of the visit as a regular office visit. This does not mean that office visit is not covered but depending on your insurance plan, it could result in a copay or co-insurance. To take advantage of your preventative health benefit, we recommend discussing other health concerns in a separate visit.
Our team of experts

children.text: Primary Care at Tia is women-focused, expanding beyond gynecological practices. We require a
whole-health practice

to broaden the collective definition of women’s health and well-being. Women’s health is a whole lot more than reproductive wellness or gynecology and it necessitates a whole lot more than an annual Pap test.
Whole Women's Health
Women’s health is more than gynecology.

At present, our healthcare system is organized by specialists whose expertise lie not in comprehensive or preventative healthcare, but in the singular management or intervention of disease. This creates a fragmented health system for patients, and particular for women.
In an effort to address this schism Tia is organized to address all of your health needs
under one roof

with your primary care and gynecology fully integrated. With this system we are able to diagnose, treat and prevent disease in women’s health. We do this through cultivating comprehensive women’s health providers who are trained to connect all of the dots that comprise female health and its nuances, complexity and interconnectedness.
Did you know?
Your Primary Care at Tia looks at the whole you, considering your unique milleui of female hormones.
